title: Noah Kahan
description: Noah Kahan, with special guest Gigi Perez, is bringing The Great Divide North American Tour 2026 to Petco Park on Monday, August 17! Two-time GRAMMY®-nominated, multi-platinum Vermont singer-songwriter Noah Kahan has risen from his New England roots to become a globally resonant voice of his generation. Across his career, Kahan has amassed nearly 15 billion global streams, sold nearly 12 million global albums, and earned 22 Platinum and Gold certifications. In addition to receiving two GRAMMY® nominations, he's been recognized by the BRIT Awards, Billboard Music Awards, iHeart Radio Music Awards, People's Choice Awards, Americana Honors & Awards, and more. Kahan's critically acclaimed breakthrough 2022 album Stick Season became a cultural phenomenon. Over the span of two years, the 4x Platinum album and its 8x Platinum title track reached number one across North America, the UK, and Europe, becoming the fourth best-selling album of 2024 and remaining in the Top 15 more than three years after its release. In 2023 following the release of Stick Season, Kahan launched his mental health non-profit, The Busyhead Project, which has raised over $5.5 million to date in support of its mission to increase awareness around the importance of mental health. Now, Kahan enters his next artistic era with The Great Divide, his fourth studio album, due April 24th via Mercury Records. Written in the wake of his rapid rise to global fame, the album finds Kahan at his most honest, reflective, and emotionally resonant—grappling with success, identity, family, distance, and the unspoken truths that separate people from one another. Produced by acclaimed producer and Stick Season collaborator Gabe Simon (Dua Lipa, Lana Del Rey, Koe Wetzel) alongside GRAMMY®-winning producer Aaron Dessner (Taylor Swift, Bon Iver), The Great Divide builds on Kahan's signature storytelling while pushing his sound into more expansive, anthemic, and sonically adventurous territory. In support of the new album, Kahan will embark on The Great Divide Tour this summer, headlining stadiums across North America for his biggest tour to date. At the heart of Noah Kahan's music remains the same guiding purpose that first drew fans in: honest storytelling, shared vulnerability, and songs that help listeners feel understood.

title: Bee San Diego — Native Bee Poker Run & Conservation Campaign
description: San Diego County is home to over 700 native bee species the highest diversity anywhere in the continental United States. Most San Diegans have never heard of a single one. Bee San Diego is a first-of-its-kind city-wide conservation campaign launching May 30, 2026, connecting local small businesses across Old Town, Barrio Logan, and Balboa Park to celebrate San Diego's native bees and fund a permanent native bee sanctuary at WorldBeat Cultural Center. Bee San Diego meets San Diegans through the things they already love — good food, coffee, a tattoo — and walks them from one neighborhood to the next, ending at a native bee sanctuary most of them never knew existed. The San Diego Bee Trail Poker Run The heart of Bee San Diego is the San Diego Bee Trail Poker Run — a self-guided city adventure that takes participants from Old Town to Barrio Logan to Balboa Park, collecting exclusive native bee illustration cards along the way. Each confirmed trail stop holds one exclusive card, available only in person. Visit every stop, collect every card, and earn a free sixth card featuring a mystery endangered California bee species, whose identity is kept completely secret until the first person completes the trail and claims their card at WorldBeat Cultural Center. Every card purchase donates directly to the native bee sanctuary. Confirmed Trail Stops Six Native Bees. Six Stories. Six San Diego native bee species have been selected from over 700 documented in the county and illustrated by campaign creative director Meg Knobel. Each bee is revealed through partner storytelling, one business, one bee, one chapter at a time in a pre-launch video series beginning in March 2026. The sixth bee's identity remains a secret until the trail is complete. Ink For Pollinators- Any bee card can be redeemed for a tattoo which will also donate a portion to the campaign. Where the Funds Go Proceeds support the WorldBeat Cultural Center Native Bee Sanctuary in Balboa Park — a permanent public native bee habitat in San Diego's most visited park, designed to support year-round pollinator activity and serve as an outdoor classroom for K-12 programs, community outreach and workshops. About Bee San Diego Bee San Diego is organized by SAVAGE BEE-CHES, San Diego's only honey boutique, located in Old Town San Diego. Woman-, minority-, and veteran-owned. The campaign is a partnership with Outdoor Traditions tattoo studio, Julia Mae's Kitchen, and WorldBeat Cultural Center, with additional community business partners joining throughout the campaign window. This campaign is open to any San Diego business that wants to be part of the story. Interested in joining as a trail partner or community sponsor? Contact us at info@savagebee-ches.boutique. Campaign website: savagebee-ches.boutique/pages/bee-san-diegoHashtags: #BeeSanDiego · #InkForPollinators · #SanDiegoBeeTrail

Participants must be 18 or older to compete in the SUPer Hero Show Down. description: <p>THE BEST PADRES PRE-GAME BAR NEAR PETCO PARK Looking for the best Padres pre-game bar near Petco Park? Garage Kitchen + Bar in the Gaslamp Quarter is just a short […]</p>\n

title: Dueling Pianos San Diego Wednesday Night | Live Music at The Shout! House | Entertainers: Dave, Jon, Jeff
description: <p>WEDNESDAY NIGHT – Doors 7pm | Pianos 8pm | 21+ Looking for the best dueling pianos show in San Diego? Every Wednesday night, The Shout! House in the Gaslamp Quarter […]</p>\n

title: Ocean Paradise
description: <p>Thousands of miles from civilization lie the keys to safeguarding our planet—three untouched atolls that give us insight into human impacts on Earth. Join us on a journey across wild tropics, pristine shores, and thriving reefs few will ever <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/ocean-paradise-2/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n
venue: San Diego Natural History Museum (The Nat)

title: Brett Dennen
description: Brett Dennen is a folk-pop singer and songwriter from Central California who was named by Rolling Stone magazine as an "Artist to Watch" in 2008. He has appeared on shows like The Late Show with David Letterman with hit singles such as "Ain't No Reason," "Cassidy," and "Ain't Gonna Lose You," which was featured on Grey's Anatomy.

title: You Should Know Podcast
description: You Should Know Podcast features candid conversations and interviews that dive deep into personal stories and current issues. The show blends humor and insight to connect with listeners on a real level.

title: Dawes
description: The LA folk rock band, Dawes, whose debut album, North Hills, entered the US Heatseekers Albums Chart, and, Good Luck with Whatever, peaked at #24 on the US Billboard Album Sales Chart.

title: Iration
description: Iration is a reggae and pop group from Santa Barbara, California. All the members of the group come from Hawaii. They play a mixture of reggae, pop, and rock. They are signed under 3 Prong. Their 2010 song, "Time Bomb," became RIAA Gold-certified in July 2019.

title: Don Carlos
description: Euvin Spencer, better known as Don Carlos or Don McCarlos, is a reggae singer and composer from Jamaica. His most popular singles include "Young Girl," "World Crisis," and "Time."

title: The Fab Four
description: The Fab Four is a tribute band from California that pays homage to The Beatles. After being founded by Ron McNeil, a John Lennon mimic, the ensemble played Beatles songs around southern California in 1997. They've performed in Japan, Malaysia, France, Hong Kong, the United Kingdom, Germany, Australia, Mexico, and Brazil, among other locations.

title: Pride Party
description: Enjoy a family-friendly Pride celebration at the Museum of Us! Registered guests enjoy free admission, activities with community partners, and special guests - Queens from The Haus Of St. James. Featured activities include: Activity stations from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. Storytime and mini performances with the Queens of the Haus of St. James A cookie decorating contest, which will be judged by the Queens and guests at 12:45 p.m. All activities are available while supplies last. Learn more and register at museumofus.org/blog/pride-party-2026

title: Pop 2000 Tour Summer Concert
description: Saturday, June 27, 2026, 6:00 p.m. at Bayside Amphitheater Relive the biggest pop hits of the late '90s and early 2000s with Pop 2000 Tour hosted by Chris Kirkpatrick of NSYNC, O-Town, Ryan Cabrera, and LFO. Sing along to fan‑favorite anthems like "On the Way Down," "Summer Girls," and "All or Nothing" in a high‑energy, hit‑packed performance.

title: Karl Denson's Tiny Universe
description: Karl Denson, who hails from Santa Ana, California, is an excellent American funk and jazz saxophonist, flutist, and vocalist. He co-founded a band named Greyboy Allstars and also played with Lenny Kravitz's musical group. He grew up in San Diego.

title: Armor For Sleep
description: Armor For Sleep is a rock band from New Jersey known for blending alternative rock, atmospheric pop, and dream pop genres. Their most popular singles are, "Car Underwater," "The Truth About Heaven," and "Dream to Make Believe."

title: Andrew Santino
description: Andrew W. Santino is a stand-up comedian, actor, and podcaster from the United States who has appeared in television shows and films such as Sin City Saints, The Disaster Artist, Mixology, I'm Dying Up Here, and Dave. He played Bill Hobbs on I'm Dying Up Here.

title: Kool & the Gang
description: With their funky grooves, infectious melodies, and vibrant horn section, Kool & the Gang has remained as one of the most successful and enduring acts in music history. Their signature soulful R&B sound is showcased on well-known hits like "Jungle Boogie" and "Hollywood Swinging."

title: Trae Crowder
description: Trae Crowder is a comedian born in Tennessee who rose to fame through his viral YouTube videos featuring a character he invented called "the liberal redneck." Along with appearing on shows like Real Time with Bill Maher, he went on tours like WellRED and published a book called The Liberal Redneck Manifesto in 2016.

title: Don Toliver
description: Don Toliver is a Houston-based hip-hop artist who has rapidly become one of the most unique acts on the scene. His soulful melodic vibes have made him one of the most sought-after voices in modern hip-hop. Don Toliver has even earned major accolades for his work, including Billboard Music Award nominations for his collaboration with Travis Scott and recognition from XXL, Vibe, and Vulture. With his signature sound and critical praise, Don Toliver is here to stay.

title: Kiefer Sutherland
description: Kiefer Sutherland is a British-Canadian actor, director, producer and singer born in London, England. His most popular character is Jack Bauer from the drama series 24 for which he won the Emmy, Golden Globe and SAG awards. He also starred in movies like A Few Good Men, Phone Booth and Pompeii.

title: Jinjer
description: Jinjer is a heavy metal band from Ukraine that has been touring internationally since their first full-length album, “Cloud Factory,” in 2014. Their popular songs include, “I Speak Astronomy” and “Pisces” which was ranked #74 in the 100 Greatest Metal Songs of the 21st Century by the magazine Metal Hammer.

title: Primus
description: Hailing from El Sobrante, California, Primus is an American rock band established in 1984. Fans may recognize "Jerry Was A Race Car Driver," "John The Fisherman," and "My Name Is Mud," three of Primus' most famous songs.

title: San Diego County Fair Fourth of July Fireworks Spectacular
description: You can watch the fireworks from many spots while at the Fair, but there's no better place to view the red, white and BOOM from the Fair's Grandstand. 🎆 Avoid the crowds and secure your seat for the Fireworks Spectacular, the celebration of the United States of America and a favorite tradition at the San Diego County Fair!

title: Rancho Bernardo's 4th of July
description: Rancho Bernardo’s Spirit of the Fourth celebration in San Diego, California, offers a full day of family-friendly festivities, featuring activities and entertainment for all ages. The event culminates with a spectacular fireworks display drawing the community together for an unforgettable evening. Attendees are encouraged to join in the fun, volunteer, or support the event through donations, helping to keep this cherished tradition alive. Experience the excitement and community spirit that make this celebration a highlight of the year in Rancho Bernardo.

title: Rancho Bernardo's The Spirit of the Fourth
description: Rancho Bernardo's premier 4th of July celebration promises a day filled with family-friendly fun and evening fireworks. Attendees can participate in the festivities, volunteer to help make the event a success, or show their support through donations.

title: 4th of July Run and Ride
description: Celebrate Independence Day in San Diego, California, with the 4th of July Run and Ride, a community favorite featuring a USATF-certified 10K race, family-friendly fun runs, and both road and gravel bike rides. Participants enjoy a fast, downhill finish and are rewarded with a top-quality patriotic shirt, finisher medals, and a lively after-party with live music, a free local craft beer garden, and activities for all ages. The event supports youth scholarships and sports in Scripps Ranch, with 60% of proceeds benefiting local student athletes. Whether you’re a seasoned runner, cyclist, or just looking for a festive way to spend the holiday, this event offers something for everyone.

title: Scripps Ranch 4th of July Parade
description: The Scripps Ranch 4th of July Parade in San Diego, California, is a beloved community tradition that brings neighbors together for a festive celebration featuring creative floats, local groups, and lively performances. Participants gather in the morning for organized staging along Red Cedar Drive, with parade marshals on hand to ensure a safe and smooth experience. The parade route winds through the heart of Scripps Ranch, ending near Hoyt Park, where additional family-friendly activities are hosted by the Scripps Ranch Old Pros. Residents and visitors alike are encouraged to join in the fun, whether by marching, cheering from the sidelines, or enjoying the festivities at the park.

title: Big Bay Boom
description: Every 4th of July, San Diego becomes the ultimate destination for fireworks enthusiasts at the Big Bay Boom. The event features one of the largest fireworks displays in the country, set against the stunning backdrop of the bay. With an anticipated attendance of over 400,000 spectators, visitors can enjoy the bursts of color and light synchronized to music while taking advantage of the city's amenities and attractions.

title: Extreme Midget Wrestling
description: Extreme Midget Wrestling is a high-energy wrestling entertainment show featuring skilled little-person athletes performing high-flying moves and intense matches. Combining athleticism with humor and showmanship, it offers a unique twist on professional wrestling. The events have gained popularity for their lively atmosphere and impressive in-ring action.

title: Shinyribs
description: Shinyribs incorporates aspects of country and soul in their music. The band has six studio albums and five compilation albums. Shinyribs' single, “Our Game,” was used as the theme music for the show, Lone Star Law.

title: 5 Seconds of Summer
description: 5 Seconds of Summer, often abbreviated as 5SOS, is a pop-rock band from Australia formed in 2011. Consisting of four members, the band has gone from being popular on YouTube to becoming one of the most successful Australian musical acts in history. Along with having over 7 billion streams, they also sold more than 2 million concert tickets and 10 million albums. Their most popular singles include "Youngblood," "Who Do You Love" and "She Looks So Perfect."

title: Kurt Vile and the Violators
description: Kurt Vile and the Violators have produced a vast collection of tracks that have captured the hearts and imaginations of listeners from around the world. Tracks such as "Pretty Pimpin," "Jesus Fever," "Goldtone," and "Life Like This" mix Vile's phenomenal songwriting abilities and the Violators' unique instrumental style to bring forth a contemporary classic rock flair.

title: E-40 Summer Concert
description: Saturday, July 11, 2026, 6:00 p.m. at Bayside Amphitheater Bay Area legend E‑40 brings West Coast hip‑hop to the stage with his unmistakable style and decades of influence. Known for shaping the sound of Northern California rap, he's delivered iconic hits like "Tell Me When to Go," "Choices (Yup)," and "Snap Yo Fingers," collaborating with artists across generations.

title: Marine Band San Diego Summer Concert
description: Experience the stirring sounds of the Marine Band San Diego Summer Concert, where a 35-piece Marine ensemble honors 250 years of the U.S. Marine Corps through powerful live performances. Enjoy an evening filled with classic American music, featuring works by legendary composers like John Philip Sousa, Henry Fillmore, Robert Russell Bennett, John Williams, and George Gershwin. This event offers a unique opportunity to witness the musical talents of the Marines in a lively community setting, making it a must-see for music lovers and patriots alike.

title: Brit Floyd
description: Inspired by Pink Floyd, the British tribute band, Brit Floyd, was formed in 2011. They try to recreate the magic of Pink Floyd's music during their live shows across the globe.

title: High Fade
description: High Fade is a funk and rock band known for their groove-heavy sound and electrifying performances. Their music fuses vintage funk rhythms with modern rock and soul influences, creating a fresh yet classic feel. With high-energy shows and tight musicianship, they are quickly gaining attention in the live music scene.

title: Disney's Beauty and the Beast
description: This touring production of Disney's Beauty and the Beast brings the beloved animated classic to life on stage. Featuring elaborate costumes, dazzling sets, and timeless songs, it offers a family-friendly theatrical experience. The show continues to enchant audiences of all ages across the country.

title: Futurebirds
description: Futurebirds bring a unique blend of indie rock and country twang to the stage, captivating audiences with their energetic live shows and soulful harmonies. An unmissable live act, they've been hailed as one of the best bands in the south.

title: Light up The Cathedral
description: Light up The Cathedral in San Diego brings together LGBTQIA+ individuals, faith leaders, and allies for an uplifting interfaith celebration. The event features a special service led by Dean Penny Bridges and other community leaders, fostering unity and spiritual affirmation. Following the gathering, the cathedral is illuminated in rainbow colors, creating a powerful symbol of inclusion and pride. This event offers a welcoming space for all to celebrate faith and diversity together.

title: Young the Giant
description: Young the Giant is an indie rock group from Irvine, California, consisting of vocalist Sameer Gadhia, guitarists Jacob Tilley and Eric Cannata, bassist Payam Doostzadeh and drummer Francois Comtois. They have achieved massive success in the indie scene, with popular songs such as "My Body," "Cough Syrup," and "Apartment."

title: Beats Antique
description: Hailing from California, Beats Antique creates experimental music blending genres like world fusion and electronic. Since their formation in 2007, they have gained recognition with live shows that present performance art and tribal fusion dance. Their popular tracks include "Beauty Beats" and "Three Sisters."

title: Evanescence
description: Evanescence is a rock band founded in Little Rock, Arkansas. The band was formed in 1995. Their first album, Fallen, was released in 2003. Two of their hit singles from that album sold more than seventeen million copies, and the band won a Grammy.

title: Del Mar Opening Day
description: Experience the thrill, fashion, and pageantry of Del Mar Opening Day, where horse racing meets high society on the California coast. From exhilarating races to eye-catching hats and chic ensembles, this iconic event is a celebration of sport and style. Whether you're placing bets, sipping cocktails, or simply soaking in the electric atmosphere, Del Mar Opening Day is the ultimate kickoff to summer’s most glamorous tradition—where the turf meets the surf!

title: Ashanti Summer Concert
description: Saturday, July 18, 2026, 6:00 p.m. at Bayside Amphitheater Ashanti is a Grammy Award‑winning singer and songwriter known for her smooth vocals and early‑2000s R&B hits like "Foolish," "Rock Wit U," and "Always on Time." She rose to prominence in the early 2000s as one of the defining voices of contemporary R&B, blending soulful melodies with hip‑hop influences, and her debut album made history by topping the Billboard 200 chart, cementing her status as a breakout star and lasting influence on the genre.

title: Tower Of Power
description: Tower of Power is a Californian R&B-based horn section and band who has been performing since 1968. Eight of their songs have been on the Billboard Hot 100 including "You're Still a Young Man" and "So Very Hard to Go".

title: Coronado Rotary Club Low Tide Ride and Stride
description: The 29th Anniversary of the Low Tide Ride and Stride promises an exciting 5K course that caters to runners, bikers, and walkers alike. Post-race, attendees can look forward to lively music, a dedicated Kids Zone, vendor booths, and enticing raffle giveaways, making it a perfect summer celebration for the whole family.

title: Low Tide Ride and Stride
description: Experience the excitement of the Low Tide Ride and Stride in San Diego, California, as you run, bike, or walk a scenic 5K course starting and finishing. Bring your friends and family to enjoy a festive atmosphere with live music, a Kid’s Zone, vendor booths, and raffle giveaways after the race. Drop off your beach chairs and reusable water bottles before the event and celebrate summer together by the ocean. This fun-filled event is perfect for all ages and offers a unique way to enjoy the beautiful coastline while supporting the local community.

title: San Diego Pride 5K
description: The San Diego Pride 5K, hosted by Front Runners and Walkers San Diego, is a lively kickoff to the city’s LGBTQ Pride Parade. Participants of all ages and abilities come together for a fun run or walk through the heart of San Diego, celebrating community and inclusivity. The event regularly sells out, with proceeds supporting local LGBTQ charities. Join the excitement, get active, and make a difference while being part of one of the most anticipated Pride events of the summer.

title: Journey USA
description: Journey USA is a talented tribute band to Journey whose vocalist sang for Steve Perry. They bring back the magic of Journey's music from the '70s to the '80s live on stage.

title: Reverend Horton Heat
description: Reverend Horton Heat is the stage name of James C. Heath and the name of his psychobilly trio from Dallas, Texas. Heath is a guitarist and singer-songwriter. Heath was dubbed the "godfather of contemporary rockabilly and psychobilly" by a Prick magazine reviewer.

title: World Championship Over-the-Line Tournament
description: The Over the Line World Championships is a unique blend of sport and entertainment, offering a festival-like atmosphere on the sandy shores of San Diego. Attendees can expect a lively social environment, complete with humorous team names, a rich history of traditions, and the opportunity to meet new people while enjoying the sun and surf.

title: Low Cut Connie
description: Low Cut Connie is a rock-and-roll band from Philadelphia popular for its high-energy performances on stage. Known for singles like “Rio,” “Revolution Rock n Roll,” and “Beverly,” they made it to the 2015 Spotify Summer Playlist of President Barack Obama and became a favorite of Sir Elton John.

title: Enhypen World Tour 'Blood Saga" in San Diego
description: K-pop's unrivaled storytellers ENHYPEN have unveiled the venue and ticketing details for their upcoming world tour, ENHYPEN WORLD TOUR 'BLOOD SAGA' in Latin America, United States, UK & Europe (hereinafter 'BLOOD SAGA'). ENHYPEN WORLD TOUR 'BLOOD SAGA' in San Diego is coming to Snapdragon Stadium on July 21, 2026!

title: ENHYPEN
description: ENHYPEN is a K-pop group known for their polished choreography, striking visuals, and genre-blending sound. Their shows are high-production spectacles filled with energy and fan interaction.

title: Ambrosia
description: Ambrosia is a rock band from Los Angeles who had five Top 40 singles between 1975 and 1980. Along with creating charting singles like, "Holdin' On to Yesterday," "How Much I Feel," and "Biggest Part of Me," they have also earned multiple Grammy nominations with albums like One Eighty and Somewhere I've Never Travelled.

title: Monophonics
description: The band, Monophonics, based in the Bay Area, is known for blending psychedelic rock and heavy soul to make some timeless tunes. Their most popular singles include, “Last One Standing,” “Bang Bang (My Baby Shot Me Down),” and “La La La Love Me.”

title: Extreme Midget Wrestling
description: Extreme Midget Wrestling is a high-energy wrestling entertainment show featuring skilled little-person athletes performing high-flying moves and intense matches. Combining athleticism with humor and showmanship, it offers a unique twist on professional wrestling. The events have gained popularity for their lively atmosphere and impressive in-ring action.

title: Rainbow Kitten Surprise
description: Formed in 2013, Rainbow Kitten Surprise or RKS is an indie alternative rock band from North Carolina. Consisting of five members, the band is known for its instrumentation, lyrics and harmonies. With popular songs like "Cocaine Jesus," "Devil Like Me" and "It's Called: Freefall," it has gained over 3 million monthly listeners on Spotify.

title: South Bay 24 Hour and South Bay Mini
description: The South Bay 24 Hour and South Bay Mini challenge runners to push their limits on a 1.22-mile loop course at Montgomery-Waller Community Park in San Diego, featuring a mix of trail, grass, and bike path terrain. Participants can choose from a range of endurance options, including 24-hour, 12-hour, 6-hour, and the unique HEFFTY Half Marathon, all run counter-clockwise around the park. The event is known for its welcoming atmosphere and the thrill of overnight racing.

title: Howard Jones
description: Howard Jones is a singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist from Hampshire, England. During the '80s, he had ten Top 40 singles in the UK, including "What Is Love?" "Things Can Only Get Better" and "New Song." His 1984 album Human's Lib topped the UK Albums Chart.

title: Soulja Boy & Paul Wall Summer Concert
description: Saturday, July 25, 2026, 6:00 p.m. at Bayside Amphitheater Discover the chart‑topping impact of Soulja Boy and Paul Wall, two hip‑hop hitmakers known for their iconic tracks and lasting influence. Soulja Boy made music history by clinching the No. 1 spot on the Billboard Hot 100 with his breakout anthem "Crank That (Soulja Boy)," a track that helped define a generation of hip‑hop. Joining him is Paul Wall, the Southern rap star celebrated for his signature style and crowd‑favorite hits like "Sittin' Sidewayz" and "Grillz."

title: Silverstein
description: Canadian rock band, Silverstein, took their name from children’s book author, Shel Silverstein, whose books they read as children. They have ten studio albums, a live CD, four EPs, and a compilation album.

title: Kevin Smith
description: Kevin Smith is the groundbreaking actor and comedian who's earned his place in the entertainment world as a legend. With hilarious films like Clerks and Mallrats, Smith has surprised audiences for decades, captivating them with his clever wit and dry delivery.

title: The Fray
description: The Fray is an American rock band known for their heartfelt lyrics and piano-driven sound. Rising to fame with hits like "How to Save a Life" and "Over My Head (Cable Car)," their music blends emotional storytelling with soaring melodies. Their introspective songwriting and anthemic choruses have made them a favorite among fans of alternative rock and pop-rock.

title: Simple Plan
description: Simple Plan is a Canadian rock band formed in 1999. The band says their music style is a combination of classic punk energy and modern pop sonics. Simple Plan has toured several states in the US and Australia and they have won multiple awards for their creativity and music.

title: Santigold
description: Santigold is a genre-defying artist mixing punk, reggae, electronic, and new wave into bold, high-energy performances. Her shows are vibrant, unpredictable, and packed with creative flair.

title: Ella Mai
description: Ella Mai is an immensely talented musician, whose soulful voice and passionate songwriting captivate audiences of all ages. Her music, combining contemporary R&B, hip-hop, and classic soul, is an infectious blend that has become a global sensation.

title: Dailey & Vincent
description: Dailey & Vincent are a bluegrass, gospel and country music duo comprised of Jamie Dailey and Darrin Vincent. The duo are highly acclaimed, winning several awards including the International Bluegrass Music Association’s Entertainer of the Year Award in 2009, and several Dove Awards. They also host a popular radio show, The Dailey & Vincent Show, which features their music and guests from the bluegrass and gospel music worlds.

title: The Incomplete History of the World in Ten Pieces
description: Music lives within history—and it also creates its own. As SummerFest marks its 40th year, this season explores both: composers responding to the world around them, and the turning points that reshaped the course of music itself. From works born in moments of upheaval to rediscoveries, landmark commissions, and bold new collaborations, SummerFest celebrates the past—and the new chapters we continue to write. Part of our Synergy Series, this boundary-crossing opening night blends music, storytelling, and theatrical narration in a fast-moving journey through history. Legendary actor Simon Callow joins festival artists for a vivid time-travel adventure—from medieval chant to modern revolution—where each piece becomes a portal to a defining moment in the story of music.

title: Jordin Sparks Summer Concert
description: Saturday, August 1, 2026, 6:00 p.m. at Bayside Amphitheater Pop and R&B sensation known for her soaring voice and unforgettable hits, Jordin Sparks rose to fame as the youngest winner of American Idol and went on to dominate the charts with crowd‑favorite singles like "No Air" (feat. Chris Brown), "Tattoo," and "Battlefield." Jordin Sparks delivers a feel‑good show packed with nostalgia, powerful vocals, and sing‑along moments fans won't forget.

title: Vienna 1900
description: Vienna's Golden Age, reimagined. At the turn of the 20th century, Vienna stood at the very center of musical life, shaping the sound of an era while standing on the edge of profound change. This concert captures a city at a golden age and a world in transformation, tradition morphing into a new age.

title: What If?
description: Pergolesi's Stabat Mater—one of the most enduring sacred works ever written, and one written by a 26-year-old—is paired with other masterpieces shaped by youth, urgency, and historical circumstance, inviting us to imagine what could have been.

title: Rick Springfield
description: Rick Springfield is an Australian-American musician and actor. After being part of the pop-rock group Zoot for three years, he started as a solo artist with the 1972 single "Speak to the Sky." With the number one hit "Jessie," he won the Grammy Award for Best Male Rock Vocal Performance in 1981.

title: Eagles of Death Metal
description: Eagles of Death Metal deliver gritty, swagger-filled rock with a blend of garage, blues, and glam influences. Known for their high-energy performances and tongue-in-cheek attitude, the band brings a wild edge to the stage.

title: Corinne Bailey Rae
description: Corinne Bailey Rae is a singer-songwriter from Leeds, England. She rose to fame in 2006 with her single "Put Your Records On" and her eponymous debut album. She has won two Grammy Awards from six nominations over the years. Over four million people listen to her monthly on Spotify.

title: Death Cab for Cutie
description: Known for melodious tracks with deep lyrics and unconventional instrumentation, like, "I'll Follow You Into The Dark," Death Cab for Cutie is an alternative rock band from Washington. Their 2008 music video, "I Will Possess Your Heart," won the Best Editing award at the MTV VMAs.

title: Reshaping the Past
description: Musical history moves forward through dialogue with the past. From Stravinsky's reimagining of baroque styles to Schubert's epic Octet—written in the shadow of Beethoven—this program explores how composers absorb, reshape, and transform what came before, creating works that honor tradition while forging new paths.

title: The Beths
description: The Beths are an indie rock band from Auckland, New Zealand, known for their upbeat melodies, sharp guitar riffs, and emotionally honest lyrics. Their sound blends power pop and garage rock with touches of punk energy, anchored by lead singer Elizabeth Stokes’ warm, expressive vocals. Their live shows are energetic and heartfelt, often balancing bright, catchy hooks with introspective themes.

title: Eastern Visions
description: Encounters with Eastern music left a lasting mark on Western composers, from the sounds of gamelan heard by Debussy at the World Fair to Lou Harrison and John Cage's lifelong fascination with Javanese and Indian sounds, culminating in a newly created chamber work based on Rimsky-Korsakov's Scheherazade.

title: Sullivan King
description: Keaton Prescott is an American DJ and heavy metal artist who goes by the alias Sullivan King. His distinctive combination of EDM and electric guitar-centered heavy metal performance has caught the attention of the music press, making his live concerts one of the rare venues where ravers and metalheads meet.

title: Strangelove: The Depeche Mode Experience
description: Strangelove: The Depeche Mode Experience is a classic-era tribute band based in Los Angeles, performing a career-spanning, "best of" performance that takes the listener on a journey through time. The band touches on major moments in Depeche Mode's 40-year career. From classics to the most recent, songs from the Depeche canon are beautifully reproduced.

title: Little Feat
description: Little Feat is a legendary rock band that formed in 1969. They disbanded due to creative differences in 1979, then got back together in 1987. Little Feat's music is an eclectic mix of rock and roll, blues, country, R&B, and jazz. Since 2003, the band has thrilled fans with a performance-laden annual trip to Jamaica.

title: Point Loma Summer Concerts
description: Point Loma Summer Concerts in San Diego, California, is a beloved free concert series that has brought the community together since 2001. Families and friends gather on summer evenings to enjoy live music from a variety of talented performers in a welcoming, outdoor setting. The event also supports youth music education and offers a chance for neighbors to connect and celebrate local talent. With its relaxed atmosphere and community spirit, it’s a highlight of the Peninsula’s summer season.

title: Siddhartha
description: Siddhartha is the stage name of Mexican musician Fernando Iglesias, known for his indie rock and pop sound, with influences from psychedelic and electronic music. Siddhartha gained recognition in the Latin American music scene with his debut album "Why You?" released in 2008.

title: Roots
description: Feel the pulse of Eastern Europe in music drawn from village dances, folk songs, and centuries-old traditions. Composers channel the raw vitality of these sounds into works of sweeping drama and rhythmic fire—music that carries the spirit of the countryside onto the concert stage.

title: Too $hort & Warren G Summer Concert
description: Saturday, August 8, 2026, 6:00 p.m. at Bayside Amphitheater Experience West Coast hip‑hop royalty with Too $hort and Warren G, two legendary artists who helped shape the sound of '90s rap. Too $hort is known for his unapologetic style and iconic hits like "Blow the Whistle" and "The Ghetto," while Warren G rose to fame with smooth, G‑funk‑defining classics including "Regulate" and "This D.J." Together, they deliver a throwback‑packed performance celebrating the legacy of hip‑hop's golden era

title: Old 97's
description: Formed in 1993, Old 97's is an alternative country band from Texas who was a pioneer in the alt-country movement during the '90s. They have released top ten charting singles like "Murder (or a Heart Attack)," "Nineteen," and "King of All the World."

title: Margaritaville Run San Diego Gaslamp 10K and 5K
description: Filled with tropical energy, the Margaritaville Run San Diego Gaslamp 10K and 5K encourages runners to embrace the "it's 5 o'clock somewhere" mood throughout this carefree, lively event. Attendees can look forward to a race through vibrant city streets, all the while enjoying fun themed drinks, live music, and festival activities, creating a remarkable island-style running party.

title: Lost and Found
description: Great music sometimes slips from view—unfinished, unpublished, or simply forgotten—until history rediscovers it. From Chopin's recently uncovered Waltz to Vaughan Williams's nearly lost Piano Quintet, from a reconstructed gem by Mozart to a lush Octet by Charles Martin Loeffler recently brought back to light at the Library of Congress, this program offers the rare chance to encounter rediscovered treasures.

title: Gipsy Kings featuring Nicolas Reyes
description: Gipsy Kings featuring Nicolas Reyes bring their signature blend of flamenco, rumba, and Latin pop to the stage with passionate vocals and virtuosic guitar work. Expect global hits like "Bamboleo" and "Volare" in a lively, feel-good performance.

title: O.A.R.
description: O.A.R. (Of A Revolution) is an acclaimed American rock band known for their energetic live performances and signature blend of rock, reggae, and jam band influences. Rising to fame with the hit single "Shattered (Turn the Car Around)," O.A.R. has built a loyal fanbase through chart-topping albums like "All Sides" and "Stories of a Stranger." Renowned for their improvisational concerts and festival appearances, the band has sold out iconic venues such as Madison Square Garden and Red Rocks Amphitheatre. Their engaging stage presence and singalong anthems make O.A.R. a must-see for fans of dynamic, feel-good rock music.

title: Santana
description: Santana is a Latin Grammy-winning American rock band formed in San Francisco in 1966. The band was created by Mexican-American songwriter, Carlos Santana. The band won six Grammy awards in one night, tying with Micheal Jackson.

title: American Legacy
description: What does it mean to make history in America? At the Library of Congress, Elizabeth Sprague Coolidge transformed a national institution into a catalyst for chamber music, commissioning and championing not only American composers but many of the greatest international voices of her time. This program reflects that expansive spirit—celebrating music shaped by bold patronage, artistic exchange, and a distinctly American belief in possibility.

title: Toto
description: Formed in 1977, Toto is a rock band based in Los Angeles that has sold more than 40 million records around the world. Known for blending elements of pop, funk, soul, R&B, blues and jazz, the band has earned several Grammy Awards with singles like "Rosanna" and albums like Toto IV.

title: JAZZ @ The JAI with Sullivan Fortner Trio
description: Returning to SummerFest after last season's electrifying collaboration with Cécile McLorin Salvant, Sullivan Fortner—one of today's most celebrated jazz pianists and a recent recipient of the Gilmore Jazz Pianist Award—brings his incredible trio to The JAI.

title: The Australian Pink Floyd Show
description: The Australian Pink Floyd Show pays tribute to Pink Floyd. This tribute band has performed in over 35 countries and has sold more than 4 million tickets at concerts. Described by The Times as the gold standard, the Pink Floyd guitarist David Gilmour performed with them at his 50th birthday party.

title: Goose
description: Goose is an indie rock band from Connecticut who has built a niche audience with their head-spinning hooks and technical fireworks. They play their popular singles like "Arcadia" and "Wysteria Lane" at countless shows across the nation.

title: Out of the Shadows
description: In the shadow of the First World War, composers faced a world irrevocably changed. From Debussy's starkly ravishing two-piano piece and Bridge's elegiac lament to Poulenc's sardonically defiant wit, this program reveals how upheaval reshaped musical language—culminating in Korngold's Suite for Left Hand, written for a war amputee and transforming loss into radiant virtuosity.

title: The Complete Brandenburgs: Part I
description: A festival within the festival. Bach's exhilarating Brandenburg Concertos—bursting with color, virtuosity, and fearless invention—take center stage across two vibrant programs that celebrate the joy of collaboration. Alongside these Baroque landmarks, music by our Composer-in-Residence Paul Wiancko and our Ensemble-in-Residence Imani Winds brings fresh energy to the conversation, proving that the spirit of innovation Bach embodied is still very much alive.

title: DJ Pauly D
description: Paul D. DelVecchio Jr., known professionally as DJ Pauly D, was born in Rhode Island. He has popular singles like "Back to Love" where he collaborated with Jay Sean. He also starred on MTV's Jersey Shore from which he got his own spin-off, The Pauly D Project, and won the award for Male Reality Star two years in a row at the Teen Choice Awards.

title: Interpol
description: Interpol is a rock band from Manhattan, New York. Their debut album Turn On the Bright Lights topped Pitchfork Media's list of Top 50 albums of 2002 and was #10 on NME's list of top albums of the year. Their popular singles include "Evil," "Rest My Chemistry" and "Obstacle 1."

title: Benson Boone
description: With a voice that could make a ballroom chandelier sway, Benson Boone skyrocketed from TikTok sensation to pop's rising star almost overnight. His soulful ballads and heartfelt lyrics resonate with a generation seeking authenticity, making him a name you'll be humming long after the playlist ends.

title: RÜFÜS DU SOL
description: The band, formerly known as, Rüfüs, before internationally changing to RÜFÜS DU SOL, in 2018, is an Australian group from Sydney. The trio's album, Atlas, charted at number one in Australia. They won an ARIA Award for Best Dance Release in 2015.

title: The Little Shop of Horrors
description: The Little Shop Of Horrors is a beloved and critically acclaimed Broadway musical based on the 1960 dark comedy film by director Roger Corman. Originally premiering in 1982, the show featured an energetic, rock & roll-style score, a riveting story, and unforgettable characters, resulting in a truly enthralling production.

title: TAKEOVER @ The JAI with Paul Wiancko
description: Cellist-composer Paul Wiancko—a member of the legendary Kronos Quartet—curates a concert that puts creativity front and center. Part salon, part sonic lab, this intimate set with festival artists brings you up close to the energy of a musician actively making history now.

title: The Complete Brandenburgs: Part II
description: A festival within the festival. Bach's exhilarating Brandenburg Concertos—bursting with color, virtuosity, and fearless invention—take center stage across two vibrant programs that celebrate the joy of collaboration. Alongside these Baroque landmarks, music by our Composer-in-Residence Paul Wiancko and our Ensemble-in-Residence Imani Winds brings fresh energy to the conversation, proving that the spirit of innovation Bach embodied is still very much alive.

title: America’s Finest City Half Marathon and 5K
description: Experience San Diego, California, at its best with America’s Finest City Half Marathon and 5K, a celebrated event that showcases the city’s stunning coastal and urban scenery. The half-marathon begins at the iconic Cabrillo National Monument, guiding runners along breathtaking views of San Diego Bay, Harbor Island, and the historic Embarcadero before finishing in the heart of Balboa Park. The 5K offers a fast, flat course winding through Balboa Park, making it ideal for both seasoned runners and newcomers. Join thousands of participants each year for a memorable race through some of San Diego’s most picturesque landmarks.

title: Jason Aldean
description: The country music singer, songwriter, and producer, Jason Aldean, is best known for his quadruple-platinum album, “My Kinda Party,” double-platinum album, “Night Train,” and several platinum albums, including, “Wide Open.”

title: Noah Kahan
description: Noah Kahan is an American folk singer and songwriter signed to Republic Records in 2017. His debut EP, "Hurt Someone," was released in 2018. His fans are called "Busyheads." He released his first single in January 2017.

title: Ne-Yo
description: Grammy-winning R&B sensation Ne-Yo is renowned for his silky vocals and masterful songwriting, having penned chart-topping hits for himself and megastars like Beyoncé and Rihanna. With a knack for blending heartfelt lyrics with infectious beats, he's defined modern R&B while continually evolving his sound.

title: Swing Fore the Parks
description: Swing Fore the Parks Golf Classic Tuesday, August 18, 2026 | Steele Canyon Golf Course Join us for a day of golf, community, and friendly competition at one of Southern California's most celebrated public courses. Steele Canyon is a stunning 27-hole championship course designed by golf legend Gary Player, featuring dramatic elevation changes, scenic vineyard views, and secluded fairways, a truly memorable setting for a great cause. Every registration includes tournament play, green fees, shared carts, a player gift, on-course contests, a pre-event meal, and an Awards Reception immediately following play. Registration: Foursome — $1,000 Bonus: The first-place foursome earns automatic entry into the 2027 Undertow Charity Golf Classic at the world-famous Torrey Pines South Course, and second place secures a spot on the North Course. All proceeds support local San Diego parks, recreation centers, and community programs through the San Diego Parks Foundation. Register at 2026 Swing Fore the Parks Questions? Contact Adelle@SanDiegoParksFoundation.org Interested in Sponsoring? Contact Carri@Sandiegoparksfoundation.org

title: Yeat
description: After receiving viral online acknowledgement from Drake, Yeat gained an international following with tracks like "Monëy so big" and "Talk." With his signature auto-tuned melodies and innovative production style, the Oregon-based rapper has amassed more than ten million monthly listeners on Spotify.

title: Remade/Reborn
description: Music survives by transformation—and this program celebrates reinvention as a creative force. Thomas Adès reimagines Purcell before leading the U.S. première of a newly revised version of his miniature Concerto Conciso, and Brahms' beloved Piano Quintet—famously transformed through multiple versions—closes the program in its final, towering incarnation.

title: SummerFest at 40!
description: Past, present, and future share the stage in one jubilant night. Music Director Inon Barnatan joins festival favorite Jeremy Denk in a virtuosic opener, former Music Director Cho-Liang Lin returns for legendary composer John Williams's Quartet La Jolla—written for SummerFest—and a world premiere commission looks ahead, before the piece that closed the very first SummerFest concert brings the celebration home.

title: Pacific Classic
description: The Pacific Classic at Del Mar Racing in San Diego, California, is renowned as the track’s most prestigious and lucrative race, drawing top thoroughbreds from across the country to compete for a $1,000,000 purse. As a Grade I event and a key qualifier for the Breeders’ Cup Classic, it attracts elite horses, trainers, and racing fans for an unforgettable day of high-stakes competition. The excitement and tradition of this celebrated race make it a highlight of the summer racing season at Del Mar.

title: TVG Pacific Classic
description: The prestigious Del Mar race, the $1,000,000 Grade I Pacific Classic, is an exciting event that offers the winner an automatic entry into the Breeders' Cup Classic. Attendees can expect a thrilling atmosphere as they witness some of the finest horses compete for the coveted title.

title: Old Country, New Age
description: From the lost years of war to the exuberance of the Roaring Twenties, England's music finds a new voice. Vaughan Williams' windswept poetry and Elgar's searing Piano Quintet give way to Walton's dazzling Façade—a jazzy, theatrical burst of modern energy— as an old country steps boldly into a new age.

title: Hulvey
description: Hulvey has risen to become one of hip-hop's most raved-about new artists and has been lauded for his ability to convey raw, thought-provoking lyricism to his fans across the globe. His most recent album, May the Bridges I Burn Light the Way, earned him a spot on Spotify's RapCaviar list, and his single "Let Go" charted as one of the 40 most popular songs in 2020.

title: Schubertiade
description: Step inside the salons where Schubert's music first came to life. This evening recreates the spirit of the original Schubertiades—intimate gatherings where friends shared new songs and chamber works at close range. Songs, chamber music, and the radiant Fantasy for four hands draw us into the world of 1820s Vienna, where this music was heard for the very first time.

title: Tori Amos
description: Tori Amos is an iconic singer-songwriter who has been making waves in the music industry since the early '90s. Her unique, ethereal sound is instantly recognizable and has been described as “a beautiful explosion of emotion.” Her most beloved songs include “Cornflake Girl,” “A Sorta Fairytale,” and “Silent All These Years,” all of which have been sung and covered by many acclaimed musicians.

title: Brad Mehldau: Ride into the Sun
description: World-renowned pianist Brad Mehldau expands the language between genres with an homage to the late indie folk artist Elliott Smith, whose music inspired a generation of songwriters. Joined by Chris Thile (vocals), Blake Mills (vocals), Matt Chamberlain (drums), and John Davis (bass), this lauded band is backed by the 17-piece USC Thornton Chamber Virtuosi on Mehldau's rich cinematic arrangements.

title: Flicks on the Bricks
description: Flicks on the Bricks in La Jolla, San Diego, transforms summer evenings into a celebration of classic cinema under the stars. Hosted by KPBS film critic and Cinema Junkie podcast host Beth Accomando, this outdoor movie series features beloved films, complimentary popcorn, and a lively atmosphere. Attendees can purchase a variety of beverages and participate in weekly trivia for a chance to win prizes. It’s a perfect way to enjoy film, community, and the unique charm of the Athenaeum’s patio setting.

title: Theresa Caputo
description: Theresa Caputo is an American psychic medium who is most well known for her reality series Long Island Medium on TLC. She has also written books named There's More to Life Than This and You Can't Make This Stuff Up.

title: The Roots
description: The Roots are an American hip-hop group led by renowned MC and producer Tariq Trotter (aka Black Thought) and drummer/producer Questlove. Hailing from Philadelphia, The Roots formed in 1987, and have since established themselves as a pioneering force in hip-hop and alternative music. The Roots have often pushed the boundaries of traditional hip-hop and collaborated with a wide range of artists, ranging from rap icons like Erykah Badu and Common, to rock legends like Elvis Costello and Jim James of My Morning Jacket.

title: Conversation Across Centuries
description: From Shakespeare-inspired song to Beethoven's haunting "Ghost" Trio, composers engage the voices of the past. The evening culminates in Vaughan Williams' luminous Fantasia on a Theme by Thomas Tallis—a rare chance to hear this soaring work performed by a specially assembled large ensemble in all its radiant breadth.

title: Brad Williams
description: Brad Williams is a distinguished comedian who has been making waves in the entertainment industry with his uncanny ability to connect with audiences of all ages. He has graced the stages of comedy venues across the country for the past decade, from HBO and Netflix specials to Comedy Central and numerous sold-out theaters. His clever wit, coupled with unique and imaginative stories, has garnered him critical acclaim.

title: Built To Spill
description: Based in Idaho, Built To Spill is an indie rock band formed in 1992. Known for long complex song structures and the guitar tunes of Doug Martsch, the band has built a loyal fanbase with singles like "Carry the Zero," "Liar" and "Goin' Against Your Mind."

title: Thee Sacred Souls
description: Thee Sacred Souls offers a unique blend of soulful R&B and funk, enhanced with their distinct vocal harmonies. From their chart-topping hits like "Can't Stop Thinking About You" and "Let's Stay Together" to their soulful renditions of classic favorites like "How Sweet It Is (To Be Loved By You)" and "Ain't No Sunshine," Thee Sacred Souls have become a staple in the music industry.

title: Rule of Three
description: For centuries, artists have understood the power of three—a principle woven throughout history in art, storytelling, and music. This exuberant finale embraces that timeless idea, pairing chamber works and concertos built around trios of soloists in a joyful, virtuosic celebration to close the season.

title: Led Zepagain: A Tribute to Led Zeppelin
description: The hard rock band, Led Zepagain, is one of the top tribute bands to Led Zeppelin in Southern California. Performing for over 30 years, the band has more than a million downloads on iTunes, played at the Led Zeppelin Reunion Concert 2007, and was named among the World’s Greatest Tribute Bands of AXS TV in 2014.

title: BABYMETAL
description: BABYMETAL is a powerhouse of the metal scene, combining driving heavy metal with danceable, pop-inspired hooks and producing high-stakes, epic live performances. Their unique blend of heavy metal and Japanese Idol Pop has transcended international boundaries, earning them two MTV Europe Music Awards with standout tracks like "Gimme Chocolate!" Their acclaimed albums "Babymetal," "Metal Resistance," and "Metal Galaxy" showcase the evolution of this peerless band.

title: Roger Clyne and the Peacemakers
description: Roger Clyne and The Peacemakers, a dynamic and energetic rock band, bring their unique style of Southwestern rock and roll to the stage. A night of feel-good music and good vibes, they'll have you singing along to their catchy tunes and dancing the night away.

title: Los Ángeles Azules
description: Los Ángeles Azules is the only Mexican duo who has two videos on YouTube with over a billion views. Being one of the biggest names in the Latin musical genre of cumbia, some of their most popular albums include "De Buenos Aires Para el Mundo" which debuted at #8 on Billboard's Top Regional Mexican Albums.

title: Del Mar Summer Racing Season
description: Experience the excitement of the Del Mar Summer Racing Season in San Diego, California, where thoroughbred racing meets a classic "surf and turf" atmosphere. This event features a 32-day meet packed with thrilling races, lively special events, and a unique coastal charm. Fans can enjoy an afternoon of top-tier competition and entertainment. Tickets are available now for an unforgettable day at one of California’s most celebrated racing destinations.

title: Atlanta Rhythm Section
description: Atlanta Rhythm Section is a Southern rock band known for smooth grooves, tight musicianship, and classic hits. Their shows blend rock, soul, and country influences with laid-back precision.

title: J. Cole
description: J. Cole is a German-born American rapper, singer, songwriter, and record producer. Regarded as one of the influential hip-hop rappers of his generation, five of his studio albums have been certified platinum by the RIAA. His album, Forest Hills Drive, scored him a Favorite Rap/Hip-Hop nomination for the Grammy Awards.

title: W.A.S.P.
description: W.A.S.P. is a heavy metal band from Los Angeles formed in 1982. Consisting of four members, it gained notoriety with its shock rock appearance and lyrics. Their most popular singles include "I Wanna Be Somebody," "Wild Child" and "Sleeping (In The Fire)."

title: Bikini Trill
description: Bikini Trill is a pop trio formed in Los Angeles in 2017. The band is made up of a vocalist, bassist, and guitarist. Their album, Wassup We Good?, was released in 2018. Their music is a fusion of electronic, pop, and hip-hop.

title: Sub Urban
description: Sub Urban is the stage name for Daniel Virgil Maisonneuve, an American singer, producer, and composer. His song "Cradles" is among his best known track. Maisonneuve was born in Nyack, New York, to a French Canadian father and a Taiwanese mother. He began making music at age 15 while growing up in the Ridgewood, New Jersey suburbs.

title: Lettuce
description: Considered as one of the greatest modern funk bands, Lettuce has a strong fan-base spread over Chicago, New York City, San Francisco and Tokyo. Their most popular albums include the charting "Crush," and the Grammy-nominated "Elevate."

title: Kamasi Washington
description: Kamasi Washington is a jazz saxophonist from LA who has worked with the likes of Kendrick Lamar and Snoop Dogg. Since winning the John Coltrane music competition in 1999, he has won several awards and earned many nominations including Grammy, Primetime Emmy, and UK Music Video Awards.

title: The Airborne Toxic Event
description: Based in Los Angeles, The Airborne Toxic Event is a rock band formed in 2006. Known for blending rock music with orchestral arrangements, they have created charting singles like "Sometime Around Midnight," "Timeless" and "Changing."

title: Chris Tomlin
description: Chris Tomlin is a contemporary Christian music singer who has sold over seven million records. He is also a worship leader and songwriter. Some of Chris Tomlin’s hit songs include, “How Great is Our God,” “Our God,” and a cover of “Good Good Father.”

title: The New Pornographers
description: The New Pornographers is a Canadian indie rock band. They've released eight critically-acclaimed studio albums including "Brill Bruisers," "Together," and "Electric Version," which was ranked among the 100 Best Albums of the Decade by Rolling Stone.

title: The Phantom of the Opera
description: The stunning new show by Cameron Mackintosh is now on tour across North America. According to critics, this magnificent show is "bigger and better than ever before." The production has numerous thrilling special effects, including the show's iconic chandelier.

title: Ringling Bros. and Barnum & Bailey Circus: The Greatest Show On Earth
description: Prepare to be dazzled, amazed, and delighted as the greatest show on Earth comes to town. Ringling Bros. and Barnum & Bailey Circus is the iconic, time-honored tradition that will leave you breathless with its jaw-dropping performances and unforgettable memories.

title: Sturgill Simpson
description: Sturgill Simpson is a genre-defying singer-songwriter known for blending country, rock, and psychedelia. His music often explores themes of identity, politics, and personal growth. He has earned critical acclaim for his boundary-pushing albums and compelling live performances.

title: Masego
description: Hailing from Jamaica, Masego is a jazz and trap artist known for his ability to effortlessly switch between playing saxophone, producing beats, and singing. Since the release of his debut album, Lady Lady, in 2018, Masego has gained a loyal fan base and has toured extensively. He has collaborated with artists such as Anderson .Paak, GoldLink, and J. Cole, and has been featured on many major music festivals.

title: Arlo Parks
description: Anaïs Oluwatoyin Estelle Marinho, also known professionally as Arlo Parks, is a British singer-songwriter and poet. She won a BRIT award in 2021. She has also been nominated for a BET award. Her debut studio album, Collapsed in Sunbeams, was released in 2021.

title: Talib Kweli
description: The American rapper, entrepreneur, broadcaster, and activist Talib Kweli has worked with multiple A-list stars including Kanye West and Pharrell Williams. His singles "Get By" and "Quality" secured high positions on Billboard Hot 100 and US Billboard 200.

title: Elder
description: Elder is a rock band from Massachusetts formed in 2006 that specializes in the sub-genres of stoner rock, progressive rock and doom metal. Using heavy psychedelic sounds, evocative soundscapes and long-scale compositions, they create singles like "Gemini," "Illusory Motion" "Sanctuary."

title: Talib Kweli
description: The American rapper, entrepreneur, broadcaster, and activist Talib Kweli has worked with multiple A-list stars including Kanye West and Pharrell Williams. His singles "Get By" and "Quality" secured high positions on Billboard Hot 100 and US Billboard 200.

title: Jack Harlow
description: Jack Harlow, born on March 13, 1998, is a singer and rapper from the United States. He is signed to Atlantic Records' Generation Now label, which is run by Don Cannon and DJ Drama. He is also the co-founder of Private Garden, a musical collective.

title: Conrad Prebys Season
description: Experience unforgettable Friday nights at The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park, where the Conrad Prebys Summer Season brings eight spectacular concerts to San Diego Bay. Enjoy a diverse lineup ranging from a grand classical opening to iconic film and stage music, including tributes to John Williams and the Gershwins. The series also features the legendary guitar artistry of Buddy Guy, making it a must-attend for music lovers of all tastes. Set against the stunning waterfront, this event offers a perfect way to kick off your summer weekends.

title: The Infamous Stringdusters & Leftover Salmon
description: Hailing from Nashville, The Infamous Stringdusters is a progressive acoustic-bluegrass band whose debut album Fork in the Road won three awards from the International Bluegrass Music Asssociation. They also won a Grammy for Best Bluegrass Album with Laws of Gravity in 2018.

title: Killers of Kill Tony
description: Killers of Kill Tony showcases standout comedians who earned recognition on the popular Kill Tony podcast. The lineup often features a mix of fresh and rising comics delivering fast-paced, edgy sets.

title: A.J. Croce
description: Experience the music of renowned singer-songwriter A.J. Croce. His dynamic style of Americana and blues-infused rock is sure to captivate you. A.J. is the son of the legendary Jim Croce and his music carries the torch of his father's legendary songwriting. Come for a night of inspiring songs and stories from A.J. and his dad's incredible legacy.

title: Sevendust
description: Sevendust is an Atlanta, Georgia-based rock band led by bassist Vince Hornsby, drummer Morgan Rose, and guitarist John Connolly, who started the band in 1994. Lajon Witherspoon, lead vocalist, and Clint Lowery, guitarist, joined the band after their first demo. The members settled on Sevendust after a few name modifications.

title: Sevendust
description: Sevendust is an Atlanta, Georgia-based rock band led by bassist Vince Hornsby, drummer Morgan Rose, and guitarist John Connolly, who started the band in 1994. Lajon Witherspoon, lead vocalist, and Clint Lowery, guitarist, joined the band after their first demo. The members settled on Sevendust after a few name modifications.

title: Earth, Wind & Fire
description: From humble beginnings in Chicago, Earth, Wind and Fire has grown into one of the biggest bands ever. Described by Rolling Stone as ”innovative, precise, yet sensual, calculated, yet galvanizing.” They have sold over ninety million records, won several awards, and have been inducted into several Halls of Fame.

title: Matute
description: Matute is a Mexican band formed in 2007 under the direction of Jorge D'Alessio. The band's first performance was to a nine person audience at their first show called "Graduation Nights." The band pays tribute to the music of the '80s in English and Spanish.

title: Chelsea Handler
description: Chelsea Handler ticks so many boxes. She is a writer, comedian, activist, producer, actor, and has her own TV show. She had a late-night show on the E! network, “Chelsea Lately,” between 2007 and 2015. She was also a correspondent for The Tonight Show.

title: The Temptations and the Four Tops
description: The Temptations is a vocal group from Michigan who delivered most of their biggest hits during the '60s and '70s. Among many popular singles, four reached the top of the Billboard Hot 100 chart including "My Girl," "I Can't Get Next to You," "Just My Imagination (Running Away with Me)" and "Papa Was a Rollin' Stone."

title: John Cameron Mitchell
description: John Cameron Mitchell is an actor, screenwriter, playwright, and director from Texas. He starred as Joe Exotic in the 2022 Peacock series, Joe vs Carole, opposite Kate McKinnon.

title: Trapt
description: Trapt is a rock band from California that was formed in 1995. Its sound belongs to styles like nu-metal, hard rock, alternative metal, and post-grunge. With singles like "Headstrong," "Echo," and "Bring It," the band has gained over 2 million monthly listeners on Spotify.

title: Cowboy Junkies
description: Cowboy Junkies is a Canadian alternative country and folk rock band formed in 1985. They gained wide recognition with their second album, The Trinity Session, which the Los Angeles Times named among the best albums of 1988. Their most popular singles are "Angel Mine," "Anniversary Song," and "A Common Disaster."

title: Cowboy Junkies
description: Cowboy Junkies is a Canadian alternative country and folk rock band formed in 1985. They gained wide recognition with their second album, The Trinity Session, which the Los Angeles Times named among the best albums of 1988. Their most popular singles are "Angel Mine," "Anniversary Song," and "A Common Disaster."

title: Carin León
description: Sonoran musician Carin León is a singer-songwriter known for his hyper-traditional music filled with accordions, acoustic guitars, strings, bass, and snare drums. His most popular singles include "Me La Avente," "El Toxico" and "Tu."

title: Get the Led Out
description: Get the Led Out (GTLO) has captured the spirit of Led Zeppelin's recorded music and will bring it to the concert stage, from the bombastic and grandiose to the folky and mystical. The Philadelphia-based band is made up of six seasoned musicians.

title: ABBA The Concert
description: ABBA The Concert is one of the greatest tribute bands to the iconic Swedish pop group, ABBA. They reincarnate the biggest ABBA hits like, "Dancing Queen," "Mamma Mia," and "SOS" on stage to cherish the timeless music of the original legends.

title: Hot Wheels Monster Trucks™ Live Glow-N-Fire
description: Hot Wheels Monster Trucks™ Live Glow-N-Fire is an electrifying live show that brings the world of Hot Wheels to life with an exciting display of monster truck action. The show features giant trucks with glowing, fire-breathing performances, thrilling stunts, and high-flying action that will leave fans on the edge of their seats. Perfect for the whole family, this high-energy event combines the spectacle of monster trucks with a colorful, larger-than-life experience that captures the imagination of both kids and adults alike.

title: Pacific Islander Festival
description: The Pacific Islander Festival in San Diego is the largest and longest-running celebration of Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander culture in the U.S. This vibrant, community-driven event brings together participants from across the country to honor the traditions of Melanesia, Micronesia, and Polynesia through music, dance, food, and art. Organized by the Pacific Islander Festival Association, it also supports scholarships and cultural education initiatives. The festival draws thousands of visitors and has become a global showcase of Pacific Islander pride and heritage.

title: Geoff Tate
description: Geoff Tate is an American songwriter and musician who was born, Jeffrey Wayne Tate, on January 14, 1959. He gained prominence as a member of Queensrÿche, a progressive metal band that achieved success with their albums, "Operation: Mindcrime" and "Empire."

title: Billy Strings
description: Aptly named young guitarist, Billy Strings, reached the #1 spot on both Bluegrass and Heatseekers charts with his 2019 album, “Home,” which later won a Grammy. Rolling Stone called him one of the “Top 10 New Country Artists To Know” in 2017 and a "Bluegrass Prodigy" in 2018.

title: Hiss Golden Messenger
description: Hiss Golden Messenger, led by Mike (MC) Taylor and Scott Hirsch, originally from Durham, North Carolina, is an American folk music band. Hiss Golden Messenger was nominated for best Americana album at the 63rd Annual Grammy Awards in 2020. The band's musical style is indie folk, blues, alternative country, and country-rock.

title: Jack White
description: Jack White is a rock singer, songwriter, musician, and producer. Along with being part of The White Stripes and winning twelve Grammy awards, he has also produced chart-topping albums as a solo artist. His popular singles include "Lazaretto," "Love is Blindness," and "Another Way to Die."

title: Spafford
description: Brian Moss and Jordan Fairless founded the American jam band Spafford. The band is known for their astonishing improvisational ability and off-the-cuff extended jams. Spafford paints a picture in real-time each night with a unique musical palette.

title: Sammy Rae & The Friends
description: Sammy Rae & The Friends is a high-energy band that blends jazz, funk, rock, and soul into a vibrant and dynamic sound. Their music features powerful vocals, tight instrumentation, and an infectious sense of joy. With a dedicated fan base and energetic live shows, they are quickly becoming one of the most exciting bands in the indie scene.

title: Awesome '80s Run
description: Step back into the era of big hair and bold colors at the Awesome '80s Run in San Diego, California, where Ruocco Park transforms into a celebration of all things '80s. Participants of all ages and fitness levels are invited to don their best retro gear and enjoy a fun run set to the soundtrack of classic hits. The event features energetic music, creative costumes, and a lively atmosphere that captures the spirit of the decade. Whether you're racing for a personal best or just soaking in the nostalgia, this run promises a memorable experience for everyone.

title: Julieta Venegas
description: Julieta Venegas is an American-Mexican singer-songwriter, instrumentalist, and producer who can play 17 instruments including acoustic guitar, accordion, and keyboard. With her pop-rock-indie Spanish music, the singles "Andar Conmigo" and "Algo está cambiando" became #1 on the Billboard Latin chart.

title: Social Distortion
description: Social Distortion is a California punk rock band known for blending raw energy with bluesy undertones and honest storytelling. With decades of touring under their belt, they’ve built a loyal following around the world. Their live shows deliver grit, heart, and classic punk spirit.

title: The Menzingers
description: The Menzingers is a punk rock band from Scranton whose most popular albums include "After the Party," "Hello Exile" and "On the Impossible Past," which was voted Album of the Year by the Spanish magazine RockZone.

title: Empire of the Sun
description: Empire of the Sun is an Australian electronic duo known for their futuristic visuals and dreamy synth-pop hits like "Walking on a Dream." Their performances are immersive, theatrical, and visually stunning.

title: Rumours of Fleetwood Mac
description: Rumours is a tribute band to the British-American rock band, Fleetwood Mac. Since their formation in 1999, they have performed at over eight-hundred major concert events to more than 750,000 fans.

title: Jack Johnson
description: Jack Johnson is a Grammy-nominated singer-songwriter and musician known for his laid-back acoustic sound and thoughtful lyrics. Rising to fame with his 2001 debut album "Brushfire Fairytales," Johnson has since released multiple platinum albums, including "In Between Dreams" and "Sleep Through the Static." His hit songs such as "Better Together," "Banana Pancakes," and "Upside Down" have become staples of modern acoustic music. In addition to his musical achievements, Johnson is recognized for his environmental activism and philanthropy, making his live performances both entertaining and inspiring.

title: Bassem Youssef
description: Often called the "Jon Stewart of Egypt," Bassem Youssef is an Egyptian comedian, writer, producer, doctor, and media critic who hosted the satirical news program, El-Bernameg. Time magazine named him among the 100 most influential people in the world in 2013.

title: MANÁ
description: MANÁ is a Mexican rock band considered to be one of the best-selling Latin music artists of all time. Selling over 40 million records worldwide, they have won multiple Grammy Awards, Latin Grammy Awards, Billboard Latin Music Awards, and more for their work. Their most popular singles include "Oye Mi Amor," "Clavado En Un Bar," and "En El Muelle De San Blas."

title: Death From Above
description: Death From Above is a rock duo from Canada formed in 2001. Consisting of bassist Jesse F. Keeler and drummer-vocalist Sebastien Grainger, it has released many popular singles like "Freeze Me," "Trainwreck 1979" and "Romantic Rights."

title: Jonathan Van Ness
description: Jonathan Van Ness or JVN is a hairdresser, podcaster, TV personality, author, actor, and activist who rose to fame after appearing as a grooming expert on Netflix's Queer Eye.

title: Ingrid Michaelson
description: Ingrid Michaelson is a singer-songwriter and actress from New York City known for her charting singles like "The Way I Am" and "Girls Chase Boys." She also temporarily played the role of Sonya Rostova on the Broadway musical Natasha, Pierre & The Great Comet of 1812.

title: for KING & COUNTRY
description: for KING & COUNTRY is a Christian pop band, formally named, Joel & Luke. The band was formed by Australian brothers, Luke and Joel, who are based in the US. The duo won a Grammy award with their incredible second studio album, Run Wild. Live Free. Love Strong, in 2017.

title: Mastodon
description: Mastodon is a heavy metal band from Georgia formed in 2000. Inspired by the '90s ascendance of new wave American metal bands, they have created top-5-charting singles like "High Road" and won a Grammy award for Best Metal Performance with the song "Sultan's Curse" in 2018.

title: The Rocky Horror Picture Show
description: The Rocky Horror Picture Show is a cult favorite known for its eccentric characters, iconic music, and interactive audience experience. Whether presented as a screening, live performance, or themed event, it offers a bold and theatrical night of entertainment.

title: Taking Back Sunday
description: Taking Back Sunday is a legendary emo and alternative rock band known for their emotionally charged lyrics and dynamic performances. With hits like MakeDamnSure and Cute Without the 'E' (Cut from the Team), they became one of the defining bands of the early 2000s emo movement. Their mix of raw energy and introspective songwriting continues to resonate with fans old and new.

title: Deer Tick
description: Deer Tick is an alternative rock-folk band from Rhode Island who released many studio albums, including, "War Elephant", "The Black Dirt Sessions," and "Divine Providence."

title: LP
description: Laura Pergolizzi, better known by her stage name LP, is a singer-songwriter from Long Island who has written songs for iconic artists like Cher, Rhianna, Céline Dion, Christina Aguilera, and the Backstreet Boys. She has also released charting singles as a solo artist like the number one hit, "Lost On You."

title: Charli XCX
description: Charlotte Emma Aitchison, better known by her stage name Charli XCX, is a singer-songwriter from England. She rose to global fame in 2014 with the Top 10 single "Boom Clap" and after being featured on the song "Fancy" by Iggy Azalea which earned two Grammy nominations.

title: The Movement
description: The Movement is a reggae band from Columbia, South Carolina, formed in 2003. Josh Swain and Jordan Miller, the two founding members, traveled to Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, recruiting local Philadelphia musicians Jay Schmidt and Gary Jackson to form a live rhythm section.

title: Kolohe Kai
description: Kolohe Kai is the Hawaiian reggae pop musical project of singer-songwriter Roman De Peralta. After its formation in 2009, the group has created singles like "Ehu Girl," "Cool Down," and "Heartstrings."

title: Dusty Slay
description: At the age of 36, Dusty Slay became the youngest comedian to perform on the Grand Ole Opry Stage in Nashville. He also wrote an episode for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon and appeared on Jimmy Kimmel Live.

title: The Expendables
description: Formed in 2008, The Expendables are an American reggae rock band hailing from Santa Cruz, California. Using elements of punk rock, surf rock, and even hip-hop, The Expendables' sound is uniquely their own with a laid-back vibe and catchy melodies.

title: Iron & Wine
description: Samuel Beam, professionally known by the alias Iron & Wine, is a singer, songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist from South Carolina. With singles like "Naked As We Came" and albums like Kiss Each Other Clean peaking at number two on the Billboard 200 chart, he has gained over three million monthly listeners on Spotify.

title: Weezer
description: Weezer is an American rock band founded in 1992 in Los Angeles, California. Rivers Cuomo, Patrick Wilson, Brian Bell, and Scott Shriner have been with the band since 2001. Weezer released their self-titled first album, commonly known as the Blue Album, in May 1994, after signing with Geffen Records in 1993.

title: Post Sex Nachos
description: Rising up from the depths of the Midwest alternative music scene is the passionate 5-piece band, Post Sex Nachos. Combining genres from jazz, pop, indie rock, and funk, their irreplaceable sound is taking audiences young and old coast to coast.

title: Dermot Kennedy
