description: Dermot Kennedy is a singer-songwriter from Ireland. More than 12 million people listen to his music every month on Spotify, streaming hit singles like "Outnumbered," "Paradise" and "Power Over Me." He has a global fandom that includes England, Australia, the Netherlands and the USA.

title: Juanes
description: Juanes is a Colombian singer-songwriter and musician. He was once a member of the rock band, Ekhymosis, before becoming a solo artist. In 2000, his solo album, Fíjate Bien, won three Latin Grammy Awards. Juanes has reached more than 15 million in album sales worldwide.

title: Switchfoot
description: Switchfoot is a rock band from San Diego who gained recognition after four of their songs were featured in the 2002 movie A Walk to Remember. They went on to receive a Grammy for Best Rock or Rap Gospel Album with Hello Hurricane.

title: America
description: Funny enough, the rock band America was formed in London. The three band members were sons of US Air Force personnel stationed in London in 1970. They dropped their debut album, “America” in 1970, and their second album “Homecoming” was released in 1972.

title: Chris Estrada
description: Chris Estrada is a rising comedian and writer known for his relatable humor and insightful commentary on everyday life. Hailing from Los Angeles, Estrada's comedy often delves into his experiences growing up in a working-class Latino family, offering a fresh and authentic perspective. His engaging storytelling and unique comedic voice have earned him recognition in the comedy scene, making him a standout performer to watch.

title: Oingo Boingo Former Members
description: Former members of Oingo Boingo play classics such as "Dead Man's Party," "Weird Science," "We Close Our Eyes," "Only a Lad," "Just Another Day," "Stay," "Out Of Control," "Who Do You Want To Be," "Gratitude," "Private Life," and many more.

title: Marc Maron
description: Marc Maron is a stand-up comedian, podcaster, writer, actor, and musician from New Jersey. Along with appearing up to forty times on TV shows like Late Show with David Letterman and Late Night with Conan O'Brien, he has hosted radio shows and interviewed Barack Obama. Now he is best known for hosting the bi-weekly podcast, WTF with Marc Maron.

title: John Scofield
description: John Scofield is a music extraordinaire and one of the most influential jazz guitarists of modern times. He has masterfully crafted his signature sound by blending funk, blues, spoken word, and hip-hop together into his intricate jazz compositions. His most popular tracks include "I Got Faith," "A Go Go," "Hottentot," and "Hed Gibbon."

title: Gary Clark Jr
description: Gary Lee Clark Jr. is an American musician well-known for his mixture of blues, rock, and soul music, with an addition of hip-hop. He released his EP, The Bright Lights, in 2011. He won a Grammy in 2014 for his hit single, "Please Come Home."

title: Jim Henson's Labyrinth in Concert
description: Jim Henson’s Labyrinth is a cult-classic 1986 fantasy film featuring David Bowie and Jennifer Connelly. A visually stunning and imaginative adventure, the film follows a young girl navigating a magical labyrinth to rescue her baby brother. Known for its creative puppetry, whimsical storytelling, and unforgettable soundtrack, it remains a beloved part of pop culture.

title: Theo Katzman
description: Theo Katzman is a virtuosic multi-instrumentalist, singer-songwriter and producer whose musical compositions are delightfully eclectic. He is known for his genre-bending melding of jazz, funk, soul, and pop elements into a truly unique sound. His most popular songs include "Modern Johnny Sings: Classic Rock," a modernized take on classic rock, and "Angela," a soulful, jazzy love song.

title: Mom Jeans
description: The Californian emo band, Mom Jeans, has released a number of studio albums, including, "Best Buds," "Sweet Tooth," and "Puppy Love."

title: Howl-O-Scream
description: Shriek and scream as pulse-pounding horrors are around every corner during Howl-O-Scream at SeaWorld San Diego. The event features sweat-inducing haunts, exhilarating rides, an interactive bar, and a monstrous live show. Scurry feverishly through scare zones to evade the angry creatures with thousands of other attendees at Howl-O-Scream.

title: UB40
description: UB40 is a British reggae and pop band best known for their smooth, socially conscious songs and covers like “Red Red Wine.” They have sold over 70 million records worldwide. The group continues to tour with their signature blend of reggae rhythms and pop melodies.

title: The Black Angels
description: The Black Angels is a psychedelic rock band from Texas best known for their 2010 album, “Phosphene Dream,” which entered the Top 50 on the Billboard charts immediately after its release. Since their formation in 2004, they have produced very popular tracks like “Young Men Dead,” “Black Grease,” and “Bad Vibrations.”

title: Jordan Jensen
description: Jordan Jensen is a talented stand-up comedian known for her sharp wit and engaging storytelling. Her comedy often delves into personal experiences and observations on everyday life, delivered with a unique and relatable perspective. Jensen's charismatic stage presence and ability to connect with audiences have quickly earned her recognition in the comedy scene, making her a rising star to watch.

title: Ride the Point (Liberty Station)
description: Ride the Point invites cyclists to experience the rarely accessible and scenic routes of Point Loma, San Diego, offering a unique perspective of the city’s coastline and landmarks. Starting at the iconic USS Recruit in Liberty Station, this event is inspired by the legacy of Jim Krause and is dedicated to supporting pancreatic cancer research. Riders of all levels come together for a day of camaraderie, breathtaking views, and the chance to make a meaningful impact. Join fellow cycling enthusiasts for an unforgettable ride that supports a vital cause and showcases San Diego like never before.

title: Bahamas
description: Earning multiple nominations and awards for his music and lyrics over the years, the Canadian, Afie Jurvanen, aka "Bahamas," has been sharing his masterpieces with the world since 2009. “Lost in the Light,” “All the Time,” “Own Alone,” and “Way with Words,” are some of his most celebrated singles.

title: Michael Blaustein
description: Michael Blaustein is a stand-up comedian and actor known for his sharp crowd work and energetic stage presence. He co-hosts a popular comedy podcast and tours internationally. His material often mixes personal stories with absurd humor.

title: The Damned
description: The Damned is a punk rock band from England formed in 1976. It was the first British punk rock band to release a single, release an album and tour the USA. Some of their popular singles are "Neat Neat Neat", "New Rose" and "Life Goes On".

title: Marisela
description: Marisela is a Mexican-American singer whose cover of "I Know (You Don't Love Me No More)" peaked at the top spot on Billboard Latin Songs in 1990.

title: Marc Broussard
description: Marc Broussard is a singer and songwriter from Louisiana who is known for his Bayou soul style that combines funk, blues, rock, pop, and R&B. With singles like “Love And Happiness,” “Cry to Me,” and “Home,” he charted twice on the Hot Adult Top 40 Tracks.

title: Leonid & Friends
description: With many sold-out US tours, Leonid & Friends is a globally popular band consisting of talented musicians from Russia, Ukraine, Belarus and Moldova.

title: Dustin Nickerson
description: Dustin Nickerson is an American stand-up comedian known for his clean, relatable humor and engaging storytelling. His comedy often revolves around his experiences as a husband and father, tackling everyday situations with wit and charm. Nickerson's approachable style and ability to find humor in the ordinary have made him a favorite among audiences of all ages.

title: Big Bad Voodoo Daddy
description: As unique as their name, Big Bad Voodoo Daddy, or, BBVD, is a Southern Californian band focused on the unique genre of contemporary swing revival. Performing at the 1999 Super Bowl half-time show, they have also produced many hit singles, like "Mr. Pinstripe Suit."

title: Del Mar Fall Racing Season
description: The Del Mar Fall Racing Season, also known as the "Bing Crosby Season," brings thrilling horse racing action to Del Mar, California, each autumn. Fans can enjoy a blend of dirt and turf races at the historic Del Mar Racetrack, with the FanDuel Racing Turf Festival serving as a highlight of the meet. The event offers an exciting atmosphere for both seasoned racing enthusiasts and newcomers, making it a must-visit for anyone looking to experience the tradition and excitement of Southern California horse racing.

title: Scott Bradlee's Postmodern Jukebox
description: Scott Bradlee, a pianist, launched Postmodern Jukebox in 2011 as a revolving musical collective identified by the initials PMJ. PMJ is noted for rewriting popular contemporary music into historical genres, particularly early twentieth-century styles like swing and jazz. Postmodern Jukebox has over 1.4 billion views on YouTube

title: OB Holiday Parade
description: The OB Holiday Parade in Ocean Beach, San Diego, CA, is a beloved community tradition that brings together locals and visitors for a festive celebration along Newport Avenue. Featuring creative floats, marching bands, and local groups, the parade captures the unique spirit and laid-back charm of Ocean Beach. Attendees can enjoy lively performances and holiday cheer as the neighborhood comes alive with decorations and joyful crowds. This event is a highlight of the season, drawing thousands each year to experience the warmth and creativity of the Ocean Beach community.

title: Buck Meek
description: Buck Meek is a musician from Texas who has been the guitarist and the backing vocalist of Big Thief. He contributed to the group's Grammy-nominated 2019 album, U.F.O.F., and started his solo career in 2018 with the eponymous debut album. His popular singles include "Indiana," "Jonathan," and "Angels."

title: David Bisbal
description: Starting from the interactive reality show, Operación Triunfo, the Spanish singer and actor, David Bisbal, has released multiple studio albums, all of which have topped Spanish albums charts.

title: San Diego Santa Run
description: The San Diego Santa Run transforms Pacific Beach into a holiday wonderland as participants dress in Santa costumes and dash along the scenic coastline. This family-friendly 5K event spreads holiday cheer with festive music, themed activities, and a jolly atmosphere. Whether running, walking, or simply enjoying the sights, the Santa Run is a unique and fun way to celebrate the season while embracing the spirit of giving and community.

title: Nutcracker: Magical Christmas Ballet
description: Experience the enchantment of the Nutcracker: Magical Christmas Ballet, a heartwarming holiday spectacle with an international cast, whimsical puppetry, and lavish costumes and sets crafted by Europe's finest artisans. Set to Tchaikovsky's timeless score, this beloved Nutcracker tradition promises a Christmas memory that families will treasure forever.

title: San Diego Holiday Half Marathon and 5K
description: The San Diego Holiday Half Marathon and 5K offers runners a unique winter race experience with a fast, point-to-point course and a net elevation drop of over 700 feet. Participants enjoy a scenic journey through San Diego, finishing at the iconic Torrey Pines State Beach. Known for its potential to help runners achieve personal bests, this event attracts both seasoned athletes and newcomers each year.

title: Prime Nights At AVANT | Last Sunday of the Month| January - December | 5:00 pm - 9:30 pm
description: Elevate your Sunday evenings with Prime Nights at AVANT, an exclusive dining experience designed for the discerning palate. Each month, savor a thoughtfully curated menu showcasing the pinnacle of our chef's creativity, featuring rotating starters and desserts, and culminating in a perfectly roasted Prime Rib Au Jus with Yorkshire Pudding and seasonal accompaniments. Selected wine pairings are available to enhance your meal, making this experience a truly unforgettable culinary journey. Price per adult is $59++ Price not inclusive of tax or 20% service charge

title: San Diego Resolution Run 5K, 10K, Half Marathon
description: The San Diego Resolution Run in Mission Bay is the perfect way to kick off the New Year with health and motivation. Offering a 5K, 10K, and half-marathon, the event caters to runners of all levels, with a course that winds through the scenic waterfront. Known for its encouraging atmosphere and wellness-focused vibe, the Resolution Run inspires participants to start the year strong. Post-race festivities include live entertainment and wellness activities, making it an invigorating way to embrace new goals and celebrate the New Year.

title: Kids Marathon Mile at LEGOLAND
description: The Kids Marathon Mile at LEGOLAND California Resort in Carlsbad, California, invites children of all ages and abilities to experience a unique 1-mile fun run through the park before it opens to the public. Participants can run, walk, skip, or stroll along a scenic route surrounded by the playful atmosphere of LEGOLAND. The event supports Merlin’s Magic Wand, with a portion of each registration benefiting this charitable partner. It’s a memorable way for families to get active together while enjoying exclusive early access to one of California’s most beloved attractions.

title: Carlsbad Marathon & Half Marathon
description: The Carlsbad Marathon & Half Marathon in San Diego, California, invites runners to experience one of the most scenic winter races in the country. Participants enjoy miles of stunning ocean views along the historic Highway 101, with enthusiastic course support and lively entertainment throughout. Hosted at The Shoppes at Carlsbad, this boutique-style event offers marathon, half marathon, and 5K distances, making it accessible for all levels. Known for its exceptional race swag and welcoming atmosphere, it’s the perfect way to turn a holiday weekend into a memorable run-cation.

title: The Bouncing Souls
description: The Bouncing Souls are a New Brunswick, New Jersey-based punk band formed in 1989. By the time the national punk rock scene recognized them, they had reintroduced a "pogo" aspect to New Jersey punk rock by playing rapid, lighthearted tunes, a format adopted by many other local bands.

title: Women's Half Marathon San Diego
description: The Women's Half Marathon San Diego invites runners of all levels to experience a scenic course along the beautiful Mission Bay. This empowering event is designed to celebrate women’s achievements in a supportive and encouraging atmosphere. Participants can enjoy stunning waterfront views, a festive post-race celebration, and a sense of community with fellow runners. Whether you’re aiming for a personal best or simply looking to enjoy a memorable day outdoors, this race offers an inspiring and inclusive experience.

title: SKECHERS Hot Chocolate Run San Diego
description: The SKECHERS Hot Chocolate Run in San Diego invites runners of all levels to take on a 5K, 10K, or 15K course, finishing with a celebration of all things chocolate. This event is known for its lively post-race party featuring decadent fondue and sweet treats. Participants enjoy a festive atmosphere filled with energy, camaraderie, and plenty of chocolate-themed surprises. Whether you’re racing for a personal best or simply for the chocolate, this run promises a memorable and delicious experience.

title: GOVX San Diego Half Marathon and 5K
description: The GOVX San Diego Half Marathon and 5K invites runners to experience a fast, flat course that winds through downtown San Diego, the scenic Embarcadero, and the lively Gaslamp Quarter. Starting and finishing near the iconic San Diego Convention Center, participants enjoy stunning waterfront views and urban energy throughout the race. The event is known for its strong support of military and first responder communities, making it a meaningful and memorable experience for all involved. Whether tackling the half-marathon or the 5K, runners of all levels are welcome to join this celebrated San Diego tradition.

title: French 79
description: Simon Henner, better known by his stage name French 79, is a pop-electro musician and producer from France whose music was featured in the Netflix series Emily in Paris. Blending '80s synth-wave with modern melodies, he has gained international fans in Turkey, Germany and Mexico. His popular singles include "Diamond Veins," "Hometown" and "Between the Buttons."

title: Stick Figure
description: Stick Figure is a reggae and dub band based in Southern California, known for the number one Billboard albums Burial Ground, Set in Stone, and World on Fire. They also have over 1.7 million monthly listeners on Spotify.

title: Community Art Workshop
description: Great for artists of all ages. Community Art Workshops invite artists to enjoy hands-on art making experiences inspired by the thinking and creating processes of artists on view at the Museum. Artists under the age of 18 must be accompanied by an adult for the duration of the workshop.   Please join us! No reservation is ...

description: <p>Discover the fascinating world of seaweed forests on a visual adventure with artist and marine scientist, Oriana Poindexter. Seaweed forests are some of the most incredible habitats on Earth, and yet these powerful sanctuaries are often overlooked and understudied. That’s where Poindexter comes in. Blending experimental photography techniques with her background in marine sciences, Oriana <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/san-diegos-underwater-forests-with-oriana-poindexter/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

Journey
description: Journey is an iconic American rock band formed in 1973, known for their powerful ballads and energetic rock anthems. The band achieved massive success in the late 1970s and 1980s with hits like "Don't Stop Believin'," "Any Way You Want It," and "Faithfully." With their distinctive blend of rock, progressive, and pop elements, Journey has left an indelible mark on the music industry and continues to influence generations of rock musicians.

title: Gorillaz
description: Gorillaz is a Grammy-winning virtual band from England formed in 1998. This unique, quirky and colorful band explores the genres of alternative rock, art pop, hip hop and electronic. With singles like "Feel Good Inc." "Clint Eastwood" and "On Melancholy Hill," they have gained over 16 million monthly listeners on Spotify. Selling over 26 million records, they became the Most Successful Virtual Band in the world according to Guinness World Records.

title: Sumer Night Fishing
description: Grab your lantern and your friends and join us for some fishing under the stars! Please leave your music at home (we have neighbors).

title: Godsmack
description: Godsmack is a rock band from Massachusetts formed in 1995. Consisting of four members, it explores the sub-genres of hard rock, post-grunge, heavy metal, alternative metal and nu-metal in its music. Three of their albums - Faceless, The Oracle and IV - consecutively topped the Billboard 200 chart.

title: Teddy Swims
description: Jaten Dimsdale, also known by his stage name Teddy Swims, is a singer-songwriter and pop artist from Atlanta. He integrates elements of soul, funk, country, and R&B into popular tracks like "911," "You're Still The One," and "Ben on Fire."

title: San Diego Beer Week
description: San Diego Beer Week is a ten-day city-wide festival that celebrates the city's renowned craft beer scene, with hundreds of bars, breweries, and restaurants hosting special events, rare beer releases, pairings, tours, and tastings. This signature event, one of the most respected beer weeks in the country, sees new breweries debut their offerings, legacy breweries release limited-edition bottles, and over 100 breweries gather for the opening ceremony, making it a true celebration of San Diego's beer culture.

title: Juneteenth Celebration
description: <p>Visitors are welcomed onto our campus to attend our fun and festive AXIS events which are designed for the whole family. AXIS is a free, outdoor, multi-generational program that celebrates the cultural diversity of San Diego by inviting local artists and craftspeople to share their cultural traditions and heritage on the Globe’s Copley Plaza. description: <p>Celebrate the start of summer at SDMRM! title: Wendy Eisenberg, More Eaze, Phil Geraldi
Brunch at The Nat
description: Turn your museum visit into Sunday Funday with the addition of brunch at The Nat. Plan a Sunday visit that includes the full Nat experience, from bones to barnacles to brunch. After you've explored the galleries, head up to the roof to grab a bite and drink while soaking up the sunshine on our patio. When it comes to brunch, we mean business. That's why we're partnering with Johnny Rivera, local restaurateur known for starting the "Brunch Revolution" with Hash House a Go Go, Great Maple, and the viral BLT Bloody Mary. Brunch will be available 10 AM-2:30 PM on each date. Seating is open, casual, and first-come, first-served. Food and beverages may be purchased a la carte, similar to Nat at Night. The rooftop is popular, so there may be a wait at times. (Interested in reserved seating? Click here to view VIP experience options). Those looking for a different flavor of breakfast are in luck: head down to the Atrium for tacos, burritos, and bowls from The Craft Taco at The Nat. They also have the best coffee and matcha in Balboa Park, but we're a little biased. Access to the rooftop brunch requires Museum admission. All purchases support our mission to conserve nature through science and discovery.

title: Film Screening: Love Birds
description: Love Birds follows the incredible story of two scientists as they discover lesbian seagulls living on California's Channel Islands, and how their work helped shape LGBTQ+ history. The Nat and the San Diego Bird Alliance are proud to present a special screening of this award-winning film about the unprecedented discovery biologists George Hunt and Molly Warner made while studying seagulls on California's Channel Islands: so-called "gay gulls." Their findings spark both outrage and celebration in a nation grappling with the rise of the LGBTQ+ rights movement. After the screening, Love Birds director, Elliot Kennerson, will share his experience working on the project and discuss its impact on the LGBTQ+ community.

title: BOARDED! A New Pirate Adventure presented by San Diego Pirate Adventures
description: Step aboard one of the Maritime Museum of San Diego's historic tall ships for a thrilling 90-minute cruise on San Diego Bay. Experience immersive pirate theatrics as the waterfront transforms into a playground of swashbuckling fun. Your adventure may take place aboard either the San Salvador, a replica 16th-century Spanish galleon, or the schooner Californian, depending on vessel availability. Whether you're a local or a visitor, this unforgettable experience is the perfect way to explore San Diego from a unique perspective. This hilarious interactive show puts visitors right in the middle of the pirate action. Participants will be sworn in as part of the ship's crew, trained how to navigate, sword fight, bail water from a sinking ship, escape from the King's Jail, swab the deck, and learn other pirate skills. Performances are 90 minutes. Choose from two shows at 10:30 a.m. or 12:45 p.m. on select Saturdays, March 21, May 16, June 13, July 11, August 15, October 10, November 28 and December 26, 2026. Please check-in 15 to 30 minutes before your reserved show time. PLEASE NOTE: The BOARDED! Pirate Show includes simulated gunfire, which may frighten young children. If your child is sensitive to loud sounds, we recommend bringing ear plugs. Sails are not set during BOARDED! cruises on San Diego Bay.

title: Stavros Halkias
description: Stavros Halkias is a stand-up comedian and podcaster from Baltimore who has been based in New York since the early 2010s. Along with touring nationally and performing at venues such as the New York Comedy Festival, he also co-hosts the podcast Cum Town with two fellow comedians.

title: Mass Creativity Day
description: We're excited to announce the 14th annual Mass Creativity, The New Children's Museum's signature program that celebrates art, imagination, and community! This year's theme, The Worlds We Grow, invites families to imagine more just, joyful, and inclusive futures, grounded in identity, culture, and community. Here's what to look forward to: Free, artist-led community workshops in partnership with our 2026 community partners Interactive art-making that celebrates the vibrancy and creativity of our communities A grand celebration on Mass Creativity Day, happening Saturday, June 27, with music and dance performances, food vendors, community art displays, and free admission to the Museum for all!

title: Kid Cudi
description: Kid Cudi is a rapper and singer known for his introspective lyrics and genre-blending music. His work, including the iconic "Man on the Moon" series, has influenced a generation of artists and fans with its exploration of mental health and personal struggles.

title: Warren Zeiders
description: At barely 22 years old, Warren Zeiders exploded onto the country music scene with the first song he'd ever written, "Ride the Lightning." Earning him a contract with Warner Records, Zeiders has continued to gain notoriety with his outlaw style and signature gritty voice.

title: Stars, Stripes & SEALs: A 250th Anniversary Celebration
description: Get ready to celebrate America's 250th Anniversary in style at the Navy SEAL Museum San Diego! We're throwing a free outdoor celebration on the waterfront packed with family fun, food, and unforgettable experiences. Enjoy complimentary burgers and hot dogs, free snow cones, giveaways, a scavenger hunt, artifact displays, and hear firsthand stories from Navy SEAL veterans. This is one Fourth of July celebration you won't want to miss, free and open to the public. Bring the family and join us as we honor 250 years of American freedom and the heroes who defend it!

title: San Marcos Red, White, and Boom
description: San Marcos Red, White, and Boom is a lively Fourth of July celebration held at William Bradley Park in San Marcos, San Diego, bringing the community together for an evening of family-friendly fun. Attendees can enjoy a variety of lawn games, inflatable jumpers, and a tempting selection of food trucks and local vendors. Guests are encouraged to bring blankets or beach chairs to relax and enjoy live entertainment before the night concludes with a spectacular fireworks show. This event is a beloved tradition, drawing residents and visitors alike for a memorable night under the stars.

title: 4th Of July at Carnitas' Snack Shack
description: The Fourth of July will be here before we know it, and that means it's time for our Annual Carnitas Party! Extended Hours: 10:00 AM - 12:00 AM Here's what you can expect: Live DJ's throughout the day and eveningPortable bar serviceLawn games and all-day funGreat views of the Big Bay Boom Fireworks (9:15 PM) — come early for food and drinks and grab a spot!Post-fireworks nightcap with food, drinks, or bothFree to attend — no cover, no tickets, just good times.

title: Chris Stapleton
description: Chris Stapleton is a singer-songwriter, musician, and record producer from the United States. He grew up in Staffordsville, Kentucky, and was born in Lexington, Kentucky. Stapleton came to Nashville, Tennessee, in 2001 to pursue an engineering degree at Vanderbilt University but left to focus on his music career.

title: Fellow Travelers
description: Fellow Travelers is based on Thomas Mallon's novel which recently became an acclaimed Showtime miniseries. Timothy Laughlin is an idealistic college grad when he arrives in Washington DC to join the fight against communism. But a chance encounter with a handsome State Department official leads to a secret love affair and heart-wrenching dilemmas. The year is 1953, and Senator McCarthy isn't just looking for communists anymore. Hearing the score Opera Magazine calls "seductively beautiful," you'll be on the edge of your seat for the work the Chicago Tribune calls "one of the most accomplished new American operas." Performed with the San Diego Symphony Orchestra.

title: Eric Roberson
description: Eric Roberson, also known as Erro, is a singer-songwriter, rapper and music producer from Rahway, New Jersey. He earned Grammy nominations with singles like "A Tale of Two" and "Still." His albums like United Tenors, Mister Nice Guy and The Box charted on the Billboard 200 chart.

title: San Diego Pride Festival
description: The San Diego Pride Parade is one of the largest and most vibrant LGBTQ+ celebrations in the country, drawing thousands of attendees, community groups, and performers to the streets of Hillcrest. Featuring colorful floats, inspiring activists, live music, and a powerful message of inclusivity, the parade is a joyful and empowering tribute to love, equality, and diversity. As the highlight of San Diego Pride Week, this event fosters community, visibility, and unity, making it a must-experience celebration of LGBTQ+ culture and progress.

title: Overnight Adventure Aboard Star of India
description: Stay overnight on the historic 1863 tall ship Star of India, docked at the Maritime Museum of San Diego. Families join the crew in costume and character for a hands-on journey into the past — complete with sailing tasks, storytelling, and old-world accommodations.Two summertime dates to choose from! Saturday July 25, 2:00 p.m. - 8:00 a.m. the following morningSaturday August 1, 2:00 p.m. - 8:00 a.m. the following morning Step into the Life of an 1800s Sailor Become the crew of Star of India on her historic 1874 voyage as the emigrant ship Euterpe, transporting 410 passengers to their new lives. You'll work alongside our education instructors, as you get to raise sails, move cargo, and and explore areas of the ship closed to the public. Sleeping arrangements while aboard are similar to that of a traditional camping experience. Participants will sleep below on the deck among the exhibits. Activities include: Raising sails Moving cargo Learning sailor skills from the 1800s Exploring areas of the ship normally closed to the public Includes dinner and breakfast Held dockside at the Maritime Museum of San Diego Space is limited; advance registration required Boarding begins at 2:00 PM on Saturday Event ends at 8:00 AM on Sunday Ages 6-17 must be accompanied by an adult Minimum participation age is 6 years old

title: Judy Collins
description: In a career spanning over 60 years, the Grammy-winning American singer and songwriter, Judy Collins, has played with many musical genres including folk, pop, and rock-and-roll. From more than thirty albums under her name, the biggest hits are "Both Sides, Now," "Send in the Clowns," and "Cook with Honey."

title: Tab Benoit
description: Tab Benoit is a blues guitarist, singer, and musician from Louisiana who has won the highest accolade for Blues musicians: B.B. King Entertainer of the Year award, twice. He also earned a place in The Louisiana Music Hall of Fame with popular tracks like “For What It’s Worth” and “Shelter Me.”

title: The Wailers
description: The infamous reggae group, The Wailers, made history between 1972 and 1981 as the backing band for Bob Marley. Before Marley's passing, he insisted that they keep his legacy alive. The current band continues to tour around the globe with top hits like "I Shot The Sheriff" and "Get Up, Stand Up."

title: The Wailers
description: The infamous reggae group, The Wailers, made history between 1972 and 1981 as the backing band for Bob Marley. Before Marley's passing, he insisted that they keep his legacy alive. The current band continues to tour around the globe with top hits like "I Shot The Sheriff" and "Get Up, Stand Up."

title: Fred Hersch Trio
description: Fred Hersch is a seminal figure in modern jazz piano who has shaped the art form for more than three decades as an improviser, composer, bandleader, and educator. Hailed by Vanity Fair as "the most arrestingly innovative pianist in jazz" and by The New Yorker as "a living legend," he is a seventeen-time GRAMMY® nominee and recipient of major honors including the Doris Duke Artist Award and multiple Jazz Journalists Association awards. He is a devoted educator who has taught at New England Conservatory, The Juilliard School, and the New School, while giving master classes worldwide. His impact on a new generation of jazz pianists is profound, shaping artists from Brad Mehldau to Sullivan Fortner, prompting Jason Moran to call him "like LeBron James on the basketball court — perfection."

title: Linda May Han Oh Trio
description: Striking, rhythmically exhilarating jazz! Award-winning NYC-based bassist and composer Linda May Han Oh leads this dynamic trio performing The Glass Hours: a collection of works based on abstract themes of the fragility of time and life, particularly exploring the paradoxes and opposites we find ourselves struggling with within our individual and societal values. The music is striking and rhythmically exhilarating and explores a wide sonic palette blending acoustic with electric elements, offering a provocative take on contemporary improvisation and composition.

title: Air Supply
description: This soft rock duo has been churning out hits since the '80s. Since the band got together in 1975, they have had a successful career. In 2013, Air Supply was inducted into the Australian Recording Induction Association (ARIA) Hall of Fame.

title: Bryson Tiller
description: Bryson Tiller is one of the most influential hip-hop and R&B performers on the music scene today. With chart-topping songs such as "Don't," "Exchange," "Wild Thoughts," and "Run Me Dry," this tastemaker of soulful sounds brings a unique perspective that captivates both on the stadium stage and in the intimate club setting. His exceptional work was recognized with Billboard Music Awards for Top R&B Song for "Don't" and Top R&B Album for Trapsoul.

title: Military Appreciation Weekend
description: Military Appreciation Weekend Join us for our monthly Military Appreciation Weekend as we honor the courage, sacrifice, and dedication of our nation's heroes. It's a powerful way for families to come together, show support, and celebrate the spirit of gratitude. In partnership with Warrior Foundation, we invite guests to write heartfelt postcards to active-duty service members at our designated mailbox—sharing words of encouragement and appreciation. Bring the whole family and join us as we recognize the brave men and women who protect our freedom at Orca Encounter presentations all weekend long. Plus, enjoy kid-friendly activities like face painting at Wild Arctic! SeaWorld proudly supports the men and women of the U.S. armed services through its Waves of Honor military salute program. The program provides free admission to the SeaWorld® and Busch Gardens® Parks, and Sesame Place® for members of U.S. armed forces.

title: The Psychedelic Furs
description: The Psychedelic Furs are a post-punk band from the United Kingdom, formed in February 1977 in London. The Psychedelic Furs, led by Richard Butler on vocals and his brother Tim Butler on bass guitar, are among the numerous bands to emerge from the British post-punk movement.

description: Test your knowledge at Trivia Night in Wild Hare Bar Garden Lounge—every Tuesday at 6 PM. Half-off taco plates during trivia! description: Hailing from Brisbane, Australia, Young Franco is a dynamic DJ and music producer known for his vibrant blend of electronic, dance, and hip-hop music. He has gained international acclaim for his energetic live performances and hit tracks like "Drop Your Love" and "About This Thing". Franco's unique sound and collaborations with artists across genres have solidified his position as a rising star in the global music scene.

title: Camp Fashion Valley
description: Join us every Thursday from July 16 through August 6 for Camp Fashion Valley, a free summer series designed for kids and families! Each week, a different community partner brings an interactive experience to the Fashion Valley Play Area. From meeting local heroes and exploring nature to making art and discovering music, every Thursday offers something new to learn, create, and enjoy. No registration required. title: Sarah McLachlan
description: Sarah McLachlan is a celebrated Canadian singer-songwriter and musician known for her emotive voice and deeply personal lyrics. Rising to fame in the 1990s, she has produced numerous hit songs such as "Angel," "Building a Mystery," and "I Will Remember You." McLachlan's music, often characterized by its blend of pop, rock, and folk influences, has earned her multiple Grammy Awards and widespread critical acclaim. She is also the founder of the Lilith Fair, a touring festival that showcased female artists and significantly contributed to the representation of women in the music industry. McLachlan's contributions to music and her philanthropic efforts have solidified her legacy as a beloved and influential artist.

description: Grupo Frontera is a popular musical group known for their vibrant and lively performances, specializing in regional Mexican music. The band combines traditional sounds with contemporary influences, creating a unique and energetic style that resonates with audiences. Grupo Frontera's catchy melodies and heartfelt lyrics have earned them a dedicated fanbase and a prominent place in the regional music scene.

title: Santee Summer Concert Series
description: The Santee Summer Concert Series in San Diego, California, is a beloved tradition that has brought the community together since 1984. This free, family-friendly event features live music, opportunities to connect with neighbors, and special themed nights, including patriotic celebrations and a Salute to Service in partnership with the San Diego County Sheriff's Office. Attendees can enjoy relaxing summer evenings filled with entertainment and community spirit, making it a highlight of the season for residents of all ages.

title: Julian Mountain Brewfest
description: Julian Mountain Brewfest Features Local Brews, Music, and More The first annual Julian Mountain Brewfest is scheduled for Saturday, August 23, offering a lively afternoon of craft beer, cider, wine and local entertainment at Nickel Beer Company. From noon to 4 p.m., guests can enjoy tastings from a curated lineup of regional breweries and cideries at the event, held at 1485 Hollow Glen Road in Julian. Tickets are $25 and include a commemorative cup and eight tasting, plus one non-alcohol tasting. This year's event will feature brews and pours from Burning Beard Brewery, Calico Cidery, Julian Beer Co., Nickel Beer Co., San Diego Brewing Company, Smoking Cannon Brewery, Storum Ranch Cider & Wine, and Walnut Tree Ranch Vineyard & Winery. Live music will be provided by the Sun Valley String Band, adding to the festive mountain-town atmosphere. Event-goers can also browse merchandise and purchase cans from select vendors, while supplies last. Set against the scenic backdrop of Julian's hillsides, the Brewfest offers locals and visitors a chance to sample some of the region's best beverages in one of San Diego County's most charming historic towns.

A vibrant celebration of Polynesian culture, this unforgettable event includes a lei greeting, Mai Tai, all-you-can-eat buffet, an activity bag for kids, and live entertainment. Enjoy authentic island music and dance performances by the local luau group, Pride of Polynesia, featuring hula and thrilling torch dancers.

Tickets are only $10 and include an exclusive, after-hours self-guided tour of the Davis-Horton House, followed by a screening of a classic movie. Check-in time is at 6:45 p.m. Guests will have 30 minutes to take a self-guided tour of the Museum. No Museum entry allowed after 7:15 p.m. We recommend bringing jackets and/or blankets for added comfort. Popcorn and other refreshments will be available for sale. The movie will be shown on a projection system outside in our courtyard. Due to the age of the films being shown, any issues with image and sound quality will be beyond the Museum's control. Tickets are very limited, so we recommend purchasing in advance.

description: Music on Main transforms Downtown El Cajon, CA, into a lively gathering spot each Friday evening, where the community comes together to enjoy live music at Prescott Promenade. Hosted by the Downtown El Cajon Association, this event features performances from talented local bands and musicians, creating an inviting atmosphere for all ages. Attendees can explore a variety of nearby restaurants and cafés, making it easy to pair great music with delicious food and drinks. Music on Main is a beloved tradition that showcases the spirit and energy of El Cajon, drawing music lovers from across San Diego County.

title: Downtown El Cajon Music on Main
description: One of El Cajon's Premier Music Events is Back—Now on Fridays! Music on Main, formerly the Dinner & A Concert Series, returns to the heart of Downtown El Cajon, bringing the community together for a vibrant weekly celebration of music, food, and connection. Hosted by the Downtown El Cajon Association, Music on Main now takes place every Friday evening from 6:00pm to 8:00pm. at Prescott Promenade, featuring live performances from talented local bands and musicians. title: Cal State Games (Summer)
description: The California State Games, is the largest amateur sports festival in California and has been bringing Olympic-style competition to San Diego, CA, since 1988. This nonprofit event features 19 different sports each summer, welcoming athletes from across the state to compete and celebrate their achievements. The highlight is the Opening Ceremonies, presented by Kaiser Permanente, which includes an athlete parade, the Lighting of the Cauldron, and a fireworks display. The Games also host a Winter edition in San Diego, offering additional opportunities for amateur athletes to participate and grow.

description: CRSSD Festival Fall is a renowned 21+ electronic music event held each year at San Diego’s scenic Waterfront Park. Curated by FNGRS CRSSD, the festival stands out for its sophisticated atmosphere, focusing on house, techno, and indie-electronic sounds across three unique stages: Ocean View, The Palms, and City Steps. Attendees can enjoy craft beverages, diverse food options, and sweeping harbor views, all while experiencing top-tier performances in a boutique, coastal setting.

title: San Diego Beer Week
description: San Diego Beer Week is a citywide celebration of one of the most vibrant craft beer scenes in the world. Spanning breweries, bars, and restaurants across San Diego County, the event highlights the creativity and innovation of local brewers. Visitors can enjoy exclusive beer releases, pairing dinners, and interactive events that bring together beer lovers and makers. It’s a perfect way to experience the city through its award-winning brews and lively beer culture.

Audiences are treated to live performances of beloved holiday hits like “All I Want for Christmas Is You,” “We Need a Little Christmas,” and “Feliz Navidad.” Inspired by the energy and spectacle of the Rockettes, this lively production is designed to delight all ages and create lasting holiday memories. Gather your family and friends for an unforgettable celebration filled with music, joy, and seasonal sparkle.

title: Seniors Free at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park
description: Seniors are invited to enjoy free admission at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park, where guests ages 65 and older can explore a world of wildlife and adventure. From the immersive Africa Tram tour to up-close encounters with kangaroos and platypuses at Walkabout Australia, there are countless ways to connect with animals from around the globe. Special experiences, like Safari Excursions and Journey into the Wild, offer unique opportunities to learn about conservation and animal habitats.

title: Chicano Park Day Celebration
description: Celebrate the rich cultural heritage of the local Chicano community at the Chicano Park Day Celebration in San Diego, California. Held in the Barrio Logan neighborhood, the annual event features live music and dance performances, traditional Aztec dance exhibitions, and the ever-popular park-wide car show. If you want to celebrate the vibrant culture of the local Chicano community, grab a bite from one of the many food vendors, browse the arts and crafts, and stay until the end to watch the fireworks display light up the night sky.

title: 10% Off Mon-Thurs Intimate Whale Watching Tours
description: Experience the thrill of being up close to nature on a small, fast, rigid inflatable boat! We glide through the ocean on a military decommissioned boat on a wildlife safari to see whales, dolphins, sea-lions, and so much more! Our boats are low profile which allows us to get arms reach away from wildlife. On our educational, guided tours every passenger gets to enjoy 360-degree, front row views and one-on-one time to talk to the captain. With no crowds, no hassle, and a personalized tour, there is no question why Adventure Whale Watching has been given 5-star ratings by almost every explorer! With small group sizes we are able to personalize every tour to give each of our guests their own unique and memorable experience! Join us for the adventure of a lifetime! Make your reservation today!Adventurewhalewatchingsandiego.com(619) 808-2822 Who we are: At Adventure Whale Watching we specialize in fast-moving, fun boat tours on Navy Seal Rigid Inflatable Boats (R.I.B.s). We are one of the only year-round Whale & Dolphin Watching companies in San Diego! Our boats set us apart because they are low profile which creates stability so it eliminates the aggressive rocking that you experience on the bigger, slow-moving boats. Our boats are also faster than typical whale watching tours which means that we are able to cover more area allowing us to see more and get closer to the wildlife. We have a small number of passengers per boat which will ensure that the boat is never crowded and that each of the guests gets a personalized experience with 360 degree views from every seat! Educational: Our captains are also naturalists and marine biologists who will educate you about the wildlife, the boats, and give you some history about San Diego! We have NOAA Marine Mammal Identification Guides we use to help passengers learn about and identify the potential species we can see on the tour. We also have tide charts and Boating Safety Information. We also talk about the Marine Mammal Protection Act, Entanglements, and Environmental Conservation. We may even pick up some balloons or water bottles if we find garbage in the ocean to help our marine friends!Safety: Our tours are guided by United States Coast Guard certified captains and naturalists aboard military decommissioned Rigid Inflatable Boats (R.I.B.s). These low-profile boats are some of the safest boats on the water! Additionally, we offer each passenger a life vest and the choice of a snow suit or a military survival suit to keep them warm and dry while on the boat. Join Us! Winter excursions, between December 15th & March 31st, are offered daily at 8:30am, 11:30am, and 2:30pm. Summer excursions, between April 1st & October 14th, are offered daily at 8:30, 12:00pm, & 3:30pm. Fall excursions, between October 15th & December 14th, are offered daily at 8:30 & 12:00pm. Pricing: The price for 2.5 - 3 hour Public Whale Watching tours are $129 plus tax per guest. Private Whale Watching tours are offered for $775 plus tax for up to 6 passengers. Use code WEEKDAY10 for 10% Monday - Thursday tours! Please call to accommodate groups larger than 6. Reservations are required.

title: The Spongebob Musical
description: Join SpongeBob SquarePants and all your favorite Bikini Bottom characters as they come to life on a stage near you. Featuring an original score by a legendary lineup of Grammy Award-winning writers, this show will take you on a hilarious and heartwarming adventure.

From movie nights under the stars to live music, games, and community resources, there's something for everyone. title: Anuhea
description: Anuhea is a singer-songwriter from Hawaii who specializes in the genres of pop, folk, and reggae. Her most popular tracks include "Higher Than The Clouds," "Like The Way It Feels," and "Right Love, Wrong Time."

title: SeaWorld San Diego Summer Concert Series
description: Check out the amazing lineup we've assembled so far and make sure you're in the audience! Prepare for another electrifying year of throwback hits from some of the top artists of the '90s and 2000s! Summer heats up with the return of the viral throwback Summer Spectacular Concert Series, featuring a lineup of '90s and early-2000s hip-hop, R&B, and pop artists, performing every Saturday night from June through August at the Bayside Amphitheater. Confirmed artists include Ying Yang Twins, Bow Wow & Dem Franchize Boyz, the Pop 2000 Tour hosted by Chris Kirkpatrick of NSYNC (featuring O-Town, Ryan Cabrera, and LFO), E-40, Soulja Boy, Paul Wall, Jordin Sparks, Too $hort, and Warren G, with additional surprise artists to be announced. Grab your friends, your family, or come solo—just show up ready to move! Join us Saturday nights at 6:00 p.m. when beloved musical artists turn SeaWorld San Diego into the ultimate open-air throwback party. With the bay lighting up at night, you can dance, belt out every lyric, and relive the soundtrack of your favorite summers with wall-to-wall nostalgia and unstoppable energy. 🎶 Concerts are included with park admission. Upgrade to reserved seating coming soon for the best views and an enhanced concert experience. *Concerts may feature adult themes and mature language. Parental discretion is advised.*

title: Country Night
description: Based in Chicago, Country Night delivers top country hits as well as all of your favorite classics through captivating live performances.

title: Concert Series on Deck
description: Join us on deck for our Concert Series! Concerts are every Saturday in May, and then every Friday and Saturday June - September. Topwater Grill will be open for amazing food and drinks. Parking is $7 and you can redeem your parking stub on your restaurant bill for $20 or more. All concerts are from 5PM-7PM.

title: Pacific Beach Half Marathon & 5K
description: The Pacific Beach Half Marathon & 5K in San Diego, California, is a scenic and exhilarating running event set along the beautiful Pacific coastline. Participants can choose between the challenging half marathon or the fast-paced 5K, both offering breathtaking views of the ocean and vibrant beachside energy. Perfect for runners of all levels, the event combines fitness with the laid-back charm of Pacific Beach. A lively post-race celebration awaits, featuring music, food, and the iconic coastal vibe, making it a must-experience race for locals and visitors.

title: Bonnie Raitt
description: Bonnie Raitt is a blues singer-songwriter and guitarist from California. With extremely successful albums and singles like Something to Talk About and Love Sneakin' Up On You, she has earned more than ten Grammy Awards including the Lifetime Achievement Award.

title: Borrego Days Desert Festival
description: Experience the excitement of Borrego Days Desert Festival in San Diego, California, at Christmas Circle Community Park, where the community comes together for a weekend of free entertainment. Enjoy the Saturday morning flyover, lively parade, and the impressive Shine & Show Car/Vehicle Show. The festival features an expanded Kids Zone, a popular Beer Garden with extended hours, delicious food stations and trucks, and a wide variety of booths offering art, crafts, and local information. It's a fun-filled celebration perfect for families and visitors of all ages.

title: Bella Vita Fest
description: Bella Vita Fest brings the spirit of Italy to San Diego’s Little Italy, blending art, culture, food, and music into a vibrant celebration. Visitors can watch stunning chalk art come to life on the streets while enjoying authentic Italian cuisine, fine wines, and live performances. The festival transforms the neighborhood into an open-air gallery and piazza, offering a taste of Italian traditions in a lively, welcoming atmosphere. It’s a perfect way to experience both local creativity and old-world charm in one unforgettable event.

title: Mission Bayfest Reggae Music Festival
description: Mission Bayfest Reggae Music Festival takes place in the scenic coastal community of Mission Beach at Mariner’s Point Park. Surrounded by lush green spaces and offering panoramic views of Mission Bay, the festival combines the vibrant sounds of reggae music with a stunning outdoor setting, creating a laid-back and memorable experience for all attendees.

title: Kishi Bashi
description: Since leaving the band, of Montreal, and embarking on a journey as a solo artist in 2011, rock/pop indie artist Kishi Bashi has garnered an abundance of critical acclaim.

title: Black Pearl Halloween Yacht Party San Diego
description: Step aboard the California Spirit for the Black Pearl San Diego Halloween Party Cruise, a 21+ haunted yacht experience on the bay. Guests enjoy a three-hour open premium bar, gourmet sliders, and two dance floors with live DJs, all while taking in stunning skyline views. Pirate-themed costumes are encouraged, adding to the festive atmosphere, but weapons and large bags are not permitted. This exclusive event promises an unforgettable night of music, dancing, and Halloween revelry on the water.

title: Little Italy Mercato (Every Saturday)
description: Experience the Little Italy Mercato: Farmers' Market every Saturday from 8:00am 2:00pm, year-round, rain-or-shine. As San Diego County's largest weekly market, the Mercato transforms six blocks of W. Date Street into a vibrant outdoor marketplace featuring the region's finest farmers, foodmakers, and artisans. Shop the European curated style market with farm-fresh produce, pastured eggs, honey, flowers, meat, and seafood direct from California growers and purveyors. Discover award-winning olive oils, baked goods, sauces, coffees, and specialty foods alongside handcrafted goods, artisan gifts, and local art. Enjoy the picturesque Piazza della Famiglia as you taste, shop, and explore the flavors and creativity of local growers and artisans. Mangia bene! Eat well. Be well!

title: Little Italy Mercato (Every Wednesday)
description: Experience the Little Italy Mercato: Farmers' Market every Wednesday from 9:30am-1:30pm, year-round, rain or shine. The Wednesday Mercato is a growing mid-week market spanning three blocks of W. Date Street from Kettner Boulevard to State Street, featuring many of the same beloved farmers and vendors from Saturday—plus weekday exclusives. Shop farm-fresh produce, pastured eggs, flowers, honey, poultry, meat, seafood, olive oil, bread, sauces, and specialty foods direct from California growers and local foodmakers. Explore artisan crafts, gifts, and artwork in the beautiful Piazza della Famiglia and surrounding blocks. Support local agriculture and small business—farmers markets keep farmers farming. Shop twice as fresh, Wednesdays and Saturdays, at the Little Italy Mercato Farmers' Markets. Mangia bene! Eat well. Be well!

The show delivers unique, on-the-spot comedy driven by audience suggestions, ensuring each performance is different. It is a dynamic and varied improv comedy show held on Fridays at 8 pm.

description: Join San Diego FC for a summer-long soccer celebration, in partnership with Adriana's Insurance. Set along Mission Beach, the event blends ocean views, lively boardwalk energy, and San Diego's signature summer culture into a one-of-a-kind experience for fans of all ages. Throughout the 39-day run, fans will be able to fully immerse in a variety of activations, including: appearances by SDFC players, coaches, and club personalities, on-site FOX 5 talent and fan activations, live music, cultural performances, giveaways, pop-ups, interactive fan experiences, exclusive merchandise drops, and more!
